api/BacklashBot.Configuration.MarketRefr.title: Class MarketRefreshServiceConfig | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Configuration.MarketRefr.summary: Class MarketRefreshServiceConfig Configuration class for MarketRefreshService parameters. Controls refresh intervals, thresholds, and performance metrics for market data synchronization. Inheritance object MarketRefreshServiceConfig Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Configuration Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class MarketRefreshServiceConfig Fields | Edit this page View Source SectionName The configuration section name for MarketRefreshServiceConfig. Declaration public const string SectionName = "WatchedMarkets:MarketRefreshService" Field Value Type Description string Properties | Edit this page View Source EnablePerformanceMetrics Enables or disables performance metrics collection in MarketRefreshService. When enabled, measures duration, market counts, throughput, CPU time, and memory usage during refresh operations. Disable for performance optimization in high-throughput scenarios. Default: true Declaration [Required(ErrorMessage = "The 'EnablePerformanceMetrics' is missing in the configuration.")] public bool EnablePerformanceMetrics { get; set; } Property Value Type Description bool | Edit this page View Source RefreshThresholdRatio Threshold ratio for triggering additional refresh pass when refresh ratio falls below this value. This determines when to perform a forced refresh on markets that haven't been updated recently. Typical values: 0.1-0.5 (10%-50%) depending on desired refresh coverage. Used by MarketRefreshService for determining when to initiate additional refresh cycles. Declaration [Required(ErrorMessage = "The 'RefreshThresholdRatio' is missing in the configuration.")] public double RefreshThresholdRatio { get; set; } Property Value Type Description double | Edit this page View Source TimeBudgetRatio Time budget ratio for the forced refresh pass relative to the total refresh interval. This limits how much time the additional refresh pass can consume to avoid exceeding the interval. Typical values: 0.4-0.8 (40%-80%) depending on refresh interval and processing requirements. Used by MarketRefreshService to prevent forced refresh from delaying the next regular cycle. Declaration [Required(ErrorMessage = "The 'TimeBudgetRatio' is missing in the configuration.")] public double TimeBudgetRatio { get; set; } Property Value Type Description double

api/BacklashBot.Helpers.TradingCalculati.title: Class TradingCalculations | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Helpers.TradingCalculati.summary: Class TradingCalculations Provides static utility methods for various trading calculations, including technical indicators, position metrics, and mathematical operations. This class is used throughout the trading bot for computing EMA, liquidation prices, fees, ROI, and other essential calculations. All methods are designed to be thread-safe and efficient for real-time trading operations. Inheritance object TradingCalculations Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Helpers Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public static class TradingCalculations Methods | Edit this page View Source CalculateBuyinPrice(double, int) Calculates the buy-in price from market exposure and position size. Includes validation for positionSize and logging. Declaration public static double CalculateBuyinPrice(double marketExposure, int positionSize) Parameters Type Name Description double marketExposure Total market exposure in dollars. Can be negative. int positionSize Number of contracts (positive for Yes, negative for No). Must not be zero. Returns Type Description double Buy-in price per contract, rounded to 2 decimal places. Returns 0 if positionSize is zero. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentException Thrown if positionSize is zero. | Edit this page View Source CalculateEMA(List<double>, int, double?) Calculates the Exponential Moving Average (EMA) for a list of prices over a specified period. EMA gives more weight to recent prices, making it responsive to price changes. Includes validation for null prices, invalid period, and NaN/Infinity values. Declaration public static double? CalculateEMA(List<double> prices, int period, double? previousEMA = null) Parameters Type Name Description List<double> prices The list of price values. Must not be null, contain at least 'period' elements, and not contain NaN or Infinity. int period The period for the EMA calculation. Must be at least 1. double? previousEMA Optional previous EMA value for incremental calculation. Returns Type Description double? The calculated EMA value or null if insufficient data, invalid inputs, or invalid result. | Edit this page View Source CalculateEMAAsync(List<double>, int, double?) Asynchronously calculates the Exponential Moving Average (EMA) for a list of prices over a specified period. Useful for high-frequency scenarios where computation can be offloaded. Declaration public static Task<double?> CalculateEMAAsync(List<double> prices, int period, double? previousEMA = null) Parameters Type Name Description List<double> prices The list of price values. int period The period for the EMA calculation. double? previousEMA Optional previous EMA value for incremental calculation. Returns Type Description Task<double?> A task that represents the asynchronous operation, containing the EMA value or null. | Edit this page View Source CalculateExpectedFees(int, List<(int Price, int Quantity)>) Calculates the expected fees for liquidating a position. Includes validation for null orderbook and invalid values. Declaration public static double CalculateExpectedFees(int positionSize, List<(int Price, int Quantity)> orderbookLevels) Parameters Type Name Description int positionSize Number of contracts to liquidate. List<(int Price, int Quantity)> orderbookLevels List of (Price, Quantity) tuples, sorted by price descending. Must not be null, prices and quantities must be positive. Returns Type Description double Total expected fees in dollars, rounded to 2 decimal places. Returns 0 if positionSize is zero or orderbook is empty/invalid. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentException Thrown if orderbookLevels contain invalid values. | Edit this page View Source CalculateIterativeEMA(List<double>, int, int) Calculates the Exponential Moving Average (EMA) iteratively up to a specified end index. This method computes EMA for a subset of prices ending at the given index. Includes validation for null prices, invalid period, indices, and NaN/Infinity values. Declaration public static double CalculateIterativeEMA(List<double> prices, int period, int endIndex) Parameters Type Name Description List<double> prices The list of price values. Must not be null, contain valid indices, and not contain NaN or Infinity. int period The period for the EMA calculation. Must be at least 1. int endIndex The end index in the prices list to calculate EMA up to. Must be >= period-1 and < prices.Count. Returns Type Description double The calculated EMA value. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentException Thrown if inputs are invalid. | Edit this page View Source CalculateIterativeEMAAsync(List<double>, int, int) Asynchronously calculates the Exponential Moving Average (EMA) iteratively up to a specified end index. Useful for high-frequency scenarios where computation can be offloaded. Declaration public static Task<double> CalculateIterativeEMAAsync(List<double> prices, int period, int endIndex) Parameters Type Name Description List<double> prices The list of price values. int period The period for the EMA calculation. int endIndex The end index in the prices list to calculate EMA up to. Returns Type Description Task<double> A task that represents the asynchronous operation, containing the EMA value. | Edit this page View Source CalculateLiquidationPrice(int, List<(int Price, int Quantity)>) Calculates the liquidation price for a position based on orderbook levels. Includes validation for null orderbook and invalid prices/quantities. Declaration public static double CalculateLiquidationPrice(int positionSize, List<(int Price, int Quantity)> orderbookLevels) Parameters Type Name Description int positionSize Number of contracts to liquidate (positive for Yes, negative for No). List<(int Price, int Quantity)> orderbookLevels List of (Price, Quantity) tuples, sorted by price descending. Must not be null, prices and quantities must be positive. Returns Type Description double Liquidation price per contract in dollars. Returns 0 if positionSize is zero or orderbook is empty/invalid. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentException Thrown if orderbookLevels contain invalid values. | Edit this page View Source CalculateROI(int, double, double, double) Calculates the ROI amount and percentage for a position. Includes validation for prices and fees. Declaration public static (double ROIAmount, double ROIPercentage) CalculateROI(int positionSize, double liquidationPrice, double buyinPrice, double expectedFees) Parameters Type Name Description int positionSize Number of contracts. double liquidationPrice Price at which the position can be liquidated (in dollars). Must be finite. double buyinPrice Buy-in price per contract (in dollars). Must be positive and finite. double expectedFees Total expected fees in dollars. Must be finite. Returns Type Description (double Accuracy, double AvgScore) (ROI Amount, ROI Percentage) tuple, both rounded to 2 decimal places. Returns (0,0) if positionSize is zero. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentException Thrown if inputs are invalid. | Edit this page View Source CalculateTradingFees(int, double) Calculates trading fees for a given number of contracts at a price. Includes validation for inputs and logging. Declaration public static double CalculateTradingFees(int contracts, double priceInDollars) Parameters Type Name Description int contracts Number of contracts. Must be positive. double priceInDollars Price per contract in dollars. Must be between 0 and 1. Returns Type Description double Fees in dollars, rounded up to the next cent. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentException Thrown if inputs are invalid. | Edit this page View Source CalculateUpsideDownside(int, double, double) Calculates the upside and downside potential for a position. Includes validation for prices and fees. Declaration public static (double Upside, double Downside) CalculateUpsideDownside(int positionSize, double liquidationPrice, double expectedFees) Parameters Type Name Description int positionSize Number of contracts (positive for Yes, negative for No). double liquidationPrice Price at which the position can be liquidated (in dollars). Must be finite. double expectedFees Total expected fees in dollars. Must be finite. Returns Type Description (double Accuracy, double AvgScore) (Upside, Downside) tuple, both rounded to 2 decimal places. Returns (0,0) if positionSize is zero. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentException Thrown if inputs are invalid. | Edit this page View Source Convolve(double[], double[]) Convolves the input array with a kernel to produce a smoothed output. Includes validation for null arrays and invalid values. Declaration public static double[] Convolve(double[] input, double[] kernel) Parameters Type Name Description double[] input The input array to be smoothed (e.g., price frequencies). Must not be null or empty, and not contain NaN or Infinity. double[] kernel The convolution kernel (e.g., Gaussian kernel). Must not be null or empty, and not contain NaN or Infinity. Returns Type Description double[] The smoothed array of the same length as the input. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentException Thrown if inputs are invalid. | Edit this page View Source ConvolveAsync(double[], double[]) Asynchronously convolves the input array with a kernel to produce a smoothed output. Useful for high-frequency scenarios where computation can be offloaded. Declaration public static Task<double[]> ConvolveAsync(double[] input, double[] kernel) Parameters Type Name Description double[] input The input array to be smoothed. double[] kernel The convolution kernel. Returns Type Description Task<double[]> A task that represents the asynchronous operation, containing the smoothed array. | Edit this page View Source FindLocalMaxima(double[]) Identifies indices of local maxima in the input array. Includes validation for null array and invalid values. Declaration public static List<int> FindLocalMaxima(double[] data) Parameters Type Name Description double[] data The input array to search for maxima. Must not be null or empty, and not contain NaN or Infinity. Returns Type Description List<int> A list of indices where the array has local maxima. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentException Thrown if data is invalid. | Edit this page View Source FindLocalMaximaAsync(double[]) Asynchronously identifies indices of local maxima in the input array. Useful for high-frequency scenarios where computation can be offloaded. Declaration public static Task<List<int>> FindLocalMaximaAsync(double[] data) Parameters Type Name Description double[] data The input array to search for maxima. Returns Type Description Task<List<int>> A task that represents the asynchronous operation, containing the list of indices. | Edit this page View Source GenerateGaussianKernel(double) Generates a Gaussian kernel for smoothing the price frequency distribution. api/BacklashBot.Hubs.BacklashBotHub.html.title: Class BacklashBotHub | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Hubs.BacklashBotHub.html.summary: Class BacklashBotHub SignalR hub for managing real-time communication between the BacklashBot trading system and connected clients. Handles client connections, handshakes, check-ins, and message routing to the Overseer system. Provides methods for broadcasting trading data, confirming target tickers, and processing overseer responses. Inheritance object Hub BacklashBotHub Implements IDisposable Inherited Members Hub.Dispose(bool) Hub.Dispose() Hub.Clients Hub.Context Hub.Groups object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Hubs Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class BacklashBotHub : Hub, IDisposable Constructors | Edit this page View Source BacklashBotHub(IServiceFactory, ILogger<BacklashBotHub>, IPerformanceMonitor, IOptions<BacklashBotHubConfig>) Initializes a new instance of the BacklashBotHub class. Declaration public BacklashBotHub(IServiceFactory serviceFactory, ILogger<BacklashBotHub> logger, IPerformanceMonitor performanceMonitor, IOptions<BacklashBotHubConfig> config) Parameters Type Name Description IServiceFactory serviceFactory Factory for creating service instances. ILogger<BacklashBotHub> logger Logger for recording hub operations. IPerformanceMonitor performanceMonitor Monitor for recording performance metrics. IOptions<BacklashBotHubConfig> config Configuration for BacklashBotHub settings. Methods | Edit this page View Source CheckIn(CheckInData) Broadcasts check-in data from a brain instance to all connected clients, typically the Overseer. Used for periodic status updates and health monitoring of trading brain instances. Declaration public Task CheckIn(CheckInData checkInData) Parameters Type Name Description CheckInData checkInData Comprehensive data about the brain's current state including markets and configuration Returns Type Description Task | Edit this page View Source ClearConnectedClients() Clears all connected clients from the collection. Used for resetting the client state. Serves as the foundation for both managed and unmanaged market management implementations. Now includes configurable queue limits and extracted target calculation service for improved testability. api/BacklashBot.Management.CentralErrorH.title: Class CentralErrorHandler | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Management.CentralErrorH.summary: Class CentralErrorHandler Central error handling service that processes logged errors and warnings from the system, determines catastrophic failure conditions, and triggers appropriate recovery actions. This class acts as the main error coordinator for the Kalshi trading bot, handling various exception types with specific recovery strategies including market resets, connection recovery, and system restarts. Inheritance object CentralErrorHandler Implements ICentralErrorHandler Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Management Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class CentralErrorHandler : ICentralErrorHandler Remarks The error handler processes errors from a database logging queue and categorizes them as either catastrophic (requiring system restart) or non-catastrophic (handled with market resets or connection recovery). It maintains counters for error tracking and implements threshold-based catastrophic detection based on configurable error frequency parameters. Configuration options include: Error window duration for frequency monitoring Error threshold for catastrophic detection Internet connectivity check parameters (max attempts, delays) Key responsibilities: Process warnings and errors from the logging queue Handle specific exception types with targeted recovery actions Monitor error frequency and detect catastrophic conditions Maintain error statistics and timestamps Trigger market resets for transient failures Manage internet connectivity checks for connection-related errors Validate input parameters to prevent null reference exceptions Constructors | Edit this page View Source CentralErrorHandler(IMarketManagerService, IServiceFactory, DatabaseLoggingQueue, IOptions<CentralErrorHandlerConfig>, IPerformanceMonitor, ILogger<ICentralErrorHandler>) Initializes a new instance of the CentralErrorHandler class. Remarks This method performs the following operations: Dequeues all errors and warnings from the logging queue Processes warnings, converting those with exceptions to errors if necessary Handles each error based on its exception type with specific recovery strategies Monitors error frequency and triggers catastrophic failure if thresholds are exceeded Cleans up old non-catastrophic errors and clears processed queues Specific exception handling includes: MarketInterestScoreDeadlockException: Logged as handled ConnectionDisruptionException: Attempts WebSocket reconnection KnownDuplicateInsertException: Logged as handled Various market-related exceptions: Triggers market resets WebSocketRetryFailedException: Marked as catastrophic Internet connectivity issues: Marked as catastrophic Overseer connection failures: Logged as informational (not catastrophic) Unhandled exceptions: Marked as catastrophic Implements ICentralErrorHandler

api/BacklashBot.Management.CentralPerfor.summary: Class CentralPerformanceMonitor Central performance monitoring service that tracks system performance metrics, records execution times, and monitors queue depths for the Kalshi trading bot. This class provides comprehensive performance analytics including WebSocket event rates, API execution times, and queue utilization metrics with configurable alerting. ICentralErrorHandler Defines the contract for a centralized error handling service that manages system errors, warnings, and connectivity checks throughout the trading bot application. ICentralPerformanceMonitor Defines the contract for a centralized performance monitoring service that tracks system performance metrics across various components including queues, WebSocket connections, database operations, and market analysis activities. IInterestScoreService Defines the contract for a service that calculates interest scores for markets based on various trading metrics and parameters. IMarketManagerService Defines the contract for a service that manages market operations including resets, watch list monitoring, and market state management within the trading bot. IOvernightActivitiesHelper Defines the contract for a helper service that performs maintenance and cleanup operations during overnight periods when trading activity is typically lower. ISnapshotGroupHelper Defines the contract for a helper service that manages snapshot grouping operations for market analysis and data organization. ITargetCalculationService Interface for calculating optimal target number of markets to watch based on performance metrics. Provides methods to determine market count targets considering various queue limits and usage patterns.

api/BacklashBot.Management.UnmanagedMark.title: Class UnmanagedMarketManagerService | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Management.UnmanagedMark.summary: Class UnmanagedMarketManagerService Implements unmanaged market management strategy for the Kalshi trading bot. Uses fixed target watch counts rather than dynamic calculation based on performance metrics. Focuses on maintaining a stable number of markets while removing ended markets and replacing uninteresting markets with higher-interest alternatives. Includes input validation for brain configuration parameters with warning logs for invalid values. api/BacklashBot.Management.html.title: Namespace BacklashBot.Management | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Management.html.summary: Namespace BacklashBot.Management Classes BaseMarketManagerService Abstract base class providing common market management functionality for the Kalshi trading bot. Handles market reset operations, interest score calculations, and provides protected methods for adding/removing markets based on various criteria. Serves as the foundation for both managed and unmanaged market management implementations. Now includes configurable queue limits and extracted target calculation service for improved testability. BrainStatusService Service responsible for managing the initialization and status of a brain instance, providing access to the brain lock and session identifier in a thread-safe manner. CentralBrain Central orchestrator for the Kalshi trading bot system. Manages the complete bot lifecycle including startup, shutdown, market monitoring, snapshot creation, and error handling. Acts as the main coordination point between various services and components. CentralErrorHandler Central error handling service that processes logged errors and warnings from the system, determines catastrophic failure conditions, and triggers appropriate recovery actions. This class acts as the main error coordinator for the Kalshi trading bot, handling various exception types with specific recovery strategies including market resets, connection recovery, and system restarts. CentralPerformanceMonitor Central performance monitoring service that tracks system performance metrics, records execution times, and monitors queue depths for the Kalshi trading bot. This class provides comprehensive performance analytics including WebSocket event rates, API execution times, and queue utilization metrics with configurable alerting. ManagedMarketManagerService Implements managed market management strategy for the Kalshi trading bot. Uses dynamic target calculation based on performance metrics rather than fixed counts. Automatically adjusts market watch counts based on system usage, queue depths, and performance indicators to optimize resource utilization and market coverage. Includes input validation for brain configuration parameters with warning logs for invalid values. MarketManagerService Main market management service that coordinates between managed and unmanaged market management strategies. Acts as a facade that delegates watch list monitoring to the appropriate implementation based on the brain instance's ManagedWatchList configuration. Provides a unified interface for market management operations while allowing different strategies for different brain configurations. Now includes configurable queue limits and extracted target calculation service for improved testability. TargetCalculationService Service for calculating optimal target number of markets to watch based on performance metrics. Implements configurable queue limits and provides detailed logging of target calculations. UnmanagedMarketManagerService Implements unmanaged market management strategy for the Kalshi trading bot. Uses fixed target watch counts rather than dynamic calculation based on performance metrics. Focuses on maintaining a stable number of markets while removing ended markets and replacing uninteresting markets with higher-interest alternatives. Includes input validation for brain configuration parameters with warning logs for invalid values.

api/BacklashBot.Middleware.SignalRAuthen.title: Class SignalRAuthenticationMiddleware | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Middleware.SignalRAuthen.summary: Class SignalRAuthenticationMiddleware Middleware for authenticating SignalR clients in the BacklashBot trading system. This middleware intercepts SignalR method invocations and connection events to validate client credentials, ensuring only authenticated clients can access sensitive hub methods. It integrates with the database to verify client tokens and manage connection state, providing a secure layer for real-time communication between trading clients and the server. ILogger<SignalRAuthenticationMiddleware> logger Logger instance for recording authentication events and errors. api/BacklashBot.Services.BroadcastServic.title: Class BroadcastService | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.BroadcastServic.summary: Class BroadcastService Service responsible for broadcasting system status and health information to connected SignalR clients. Manages periodic check-in broadcasts at configurable intervals containing brain instance status, market data, performance metrics, and system health indicators to keep monitoring systems and dashboards updated. Includes retry logic for failed SignalR sends and performance metrics collection. api/BacklashBot.Services.CandlestickServ.title: Class CandlestickService | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.CandlestickServ.summary: Class CandlestickService Service responsible for managing candlestick data for trading markets. Handles loading, processing, and persistence of historical price data from both Parquet files and SQL database, including forward filling and deduplication. Provides market data population and candlestick synchronization functionality. api/BacklashBot.Services.Interfaces.html.title: Namespace BacklashBot.Services.Interfaces | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.Interfaces.html.summary: Namespace BacklashBot.Services.Interfaces Interfaces IBroadcastService Defines the contract for a service that handles broadcasting operations within the trading bot system, including starting and stopping broadcast services. ICandlestickService Defines the contract for a service that manages candlestick data operations, including updating, populating, and retrieving historical candlestick information for markets. IMarketDataInitializer Defines the contract for a service that initializes market data, setting up necessary data structures and connections for market data operations. IMarketDataService Defines the contract for a comprehensive market data service that manages market information, watch lists, positions, account data, and real-time updates through WebSocket connections. IMarketRefreshService Defines the contract for a service that handles market data refresh operations, including periodic updates, immediate refreshes, and performance monitoring. IOrderBookService Defines the contract for a service that manages order book data and operations, including WebSocket event handling, queue management, and synchronization. IOrderbookChangeTracker Defines the contract for a service that tracks and analyzes order book changes, providing metrics and insights into market dynamics and trading activity. IScopeManagerService Defines the contract for a service that manages dependency injection scopes and provides access to scoped services within the application. IServiceFactory Defines the contract for a service factory that provides centralized access to various services within the trading bot application through dependency injection. ISqlDataService Defines the contract for a service that handles SQL data operations, including storing various types of trading data and executing import jobs. ITradingSnapshotService Defines the contract for a service that manages trading snapshots, including saving, loading, validation, and schema management. IWebSocketMonitorService Defines the contract for a service that monitors WebSocket connections, handles connection management, and provides connection status information.

api/BacklashBot.Services.KalshiBotScopeM.summary: Class KalshiBotScopeManagerService Service for managing dependency injection scopes in the Kalshi trading bot system. This service handles the creation, initialization, and disposal of service scopes, ensuring proper resource management and access to scoped services throughout the application. Includes configurable performance metrics for monitoring scope efficiency. api/BacklashBot.Services.MarketDataIniti.title: Class MarketDataInitializer | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.MarketDataIniti.summary: Class MarketDataInitializer Service responsible for initializing market data during application startup. This service fetches watched markets, subscribes to WebSocket channels for new markets, synchronizes market data, and sets up positions and account balance. It ensures that all necessary market data is available before the application becomes fully operational. api/BacklashBot.Services.OrderbookChange.title: Class OrderbookChangeTracker | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.OrderbookChange.summary: Class OrderbookChangeTracker Tracks and analyzes orderbook changes for a specific market in the Kalshi trading system. This class is responsible for processing orderbook snapshots, recording individual changes, matching trades to orderbook changes, calculating various market metrics (velocity, volume, rates), and maintaining a rolling window of orderbook events for analysis. It implements the IOrderbookChangeTracker interface and integrates with the broader trading bot ecosystem to provide real-time market data analysis and metrics calculation. Inheritance object OrderbookChangeTracker Implements IOrderbookChangeTracker IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Services Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class OrderbookChangeTracker : IOrderbookChangeTracker, IDisposable Remarks The tracker maintains concurrent queues for orderbook changes and trade events, using timers for periodic metric recalculation and cleanup of old events. It employs locking mechanisms to ensure thread safety during matching operations and supports cancellation tokens for graceful shutdown. Metrics are calculated over configurable time windows and include velocity per minute, trade rates, average trade sizes, and order volume analysis. Constructors | Edit this page View Source OrderbookChangeTracker(string, ILogger<IOrderbookChangeTracker>, IDataCache, IOptions<OrderbookChangeTrackerConfig>, IScopeManagerService, IStatusTrackerService, IPerformanceMonitor) Initializes a new instance of the OrderbookChangeTracker for the specified market. Sets up timers for periodic metric recalculation and log output, initializes event queues, and prepares the tracker for processing orderbook changes and trades. api/BacklashBot.Services.OverseerClientS.title: Class OverseerClientService | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.OverseerClientS.summary: Class OverseerClientService Manages the client-side connection to an Overseer server for monitoring and oversight of the trading bot. This service establishes and maintains a SignalR connection to an Overseer instance, handles periodic check-ins with system status information, and automatically discovers and switches to better overseer servers when available. It provides resilience through automatic reconnection, connection validation, and failover mechanisms. The service integrates with the broader bot ecosystem to report market data, error counts, and performance metrics. Inheritance object OverseerClientService Implements IOverseerClientService Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Services Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class OverseerClientService : IOverseerClientService Remarks The OverseerClientService is designed to be optional - if no overseer is available, the bot continues operating without oversight. It discovers available overseer instances from the database only, prioritizing production instances over those named "DevInstance", and selects the most appropriate one based on recency of heartbeat. Constructors | Edit this page View Source OverseerClientService(ILogger<OverseerClientService>, IServiceFactory, IStatusTrackerService, IOptions<OverseerClientServiceConfig>, IOptions<InstanceNameConfig>, IPerformanceMonitor, ICentralPerformanceMonitor) Initializes a new instance of the OverseerClientService with required dependencies. Declaration public OverseerClientService(ILogger<OverseerClientService> logger, IServiceFactory serviceFactory, IStatusTrackerService statusTrackerService, IOptions<OverseerClientServiceConfig> overseerConfig, IOptions<InstanceNameConfig> instanceNameConfig, IPerformanceMonitor performanceMonitor, ICentralPerformanceMonitor centralPerformanceMonitor) Parameters Type Name Description ILogger<OverseerClientService> logger The logger instance for recording service operations and errors. api/BacklashBot.Services.ServiceFactory..title: Class ServiceFactory | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.ServiceFactory..summary: Class ServiceFactory Provides centralized access to various services within the Kalshi trading bot system through dependency injection scopes. This factory implements the service locator pattern to manage service initialization, configuration validation, and thread-safe access to scoped services. It acts as a bridge between the application's dependency injection container and service consumers, ensuring proper scope management and resource cleanup. Services are cached per scope to improve performance by avoiding repeated dependency injection resolution. api/BacklashBot.Services.TradingCalculat.title: Class TradingCalculator | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.TradingCalculat.summary: Class TradingCalculator Provides calculations for various technical indicators used in trading strategies for the Kalshi marketplace. This class implements the ITradingCalculator interface and computes indicators such as RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic Oscillator, ATR, VWAP, OBV, support/resistance levels, PSAR, and ADX based on pseudo-candlestick data. All calculations are designed for event-driven contracts with prices bounded between 1 and 99 cents. Depends on CalculationConfig for indicator parameters, includes async methods for performance in high-frequency scenarios, and incorporates comprehensive input validation including null checks, empty list handling, and data integrity checks with warnings. Inheritance object TradingCalculator Implements ITradingCalculator Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.Services Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class TradingCalculator : ITradingCalculator Remarks Indicators are calculated using standard formulas adapted for Kalshi's binary outcome markets. Null values are returned when insufficient data is available for reliable calculations. Input validation ensures data quality by checking for null lists, empty collections, and invalid candlestick data (e.g., negative prices, invalid timestamps). Methods | Edit this page View Source CalculateADX(List<PseudoCandlestick>) Calculates the Average Directional Index (ADX) value along with +DI and -DI for a series of pseudo-candlesticks in the Kalshi marketplace. This indicator serves as a robust complementary metric, particularly when integrated with tools like the Parabolic Stop and Reverse (PSAR) to validate trend strength. It provides an objective measure of trend intensity, irrespective of direction, which aids in distinguishing genuine trends from periods of consolidation or volatility without momentum. This is especially pertinent in Kalshi's event-driven environment, where contract prices may exhibit pronounced trends due to evolving probabilities influenced by economic announcements or other developments. By confirming the presence of a strong trend (typically ADX values exceeding 25), it enhances the efficacy of PSAR signals, reducing the likelihood of whipsaws in non-trending conditions. The +DI and -DI components provide directional information: +DI > -DI suggests uptrend, -DI > +DI suggests downtrend. Its utility diminishes in highly volatile, range-bound markets common to certain Kalshi contracts awaiting resolution. Declaration public (double? ADX, double? PlusDI, double? MinusDI) CalculateADX(List<PseudoCandlestick> pseudoCandles) Parameters Type Name Description List<PseudoCandlestick> pseudoCandles The list of pseudo-candlesticks containing mid-high, mid-low, and mid-close prices. Returns Type Description (double? MACD, double? Signal, double? Histogram) A tuple containing ADX, +DI, and -DI values, or null values if insufficient data or invalid computation. Remarks Input validation includes null check for the pseudo-candlesticks list, empty list handling with warnings, and data integrity checks with warnings for invalid candlestick data (e.g., negative prices, invalid timestamps). Parameters are sourced from configuration where applicable (e.g., ADX period). Interpretations of ADX values are as follows in Kalshi's context: Below 20: Indicates a weak or absent trend, suggesting consolidation or choppy conditions; traders should exercise caution with trend-following strategies like PSAR, as signals may prove unreliable. Between 20 and 25: Signals the potential emergence of a trend; this transitional range warrants monitoring for confirmation, such as a PSAR crossover aligned with rising ADX. Between 25 and 40: Reflects a strong trend, validating PSAR positions and encouraging trend continuation trades, with higher values denoting increased momentum. Above 40: Denotes an exceptionally strong trend, often nearing potential exhaustion; while supportive of PSAR trailing stops, it may foreshadow reversals, prompting consideration of profit-taking. +DI and -DI interpretations: +DI > -DI: Suggests upward trend strength -DI > +DI: Suggests downward trend strength +DI crossing above -DI: Potential bullish signal -DI crossing above +DI: Potential bearish signal CalculateADXAsync(List<PseudoCandlestick>) Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException Thrown when pseudoCandles is null. | Edit this page View Source CalculateADXAsync(List<PseudoCandlestick>) Calculates the Average Directional Index (ADX) value asynchronously along with +DI and -DI for a series of pseudo-candlesticks in the Kalshi marketplace. By confirming the presence of a strong trend (typically ADX values exceeding 25), it enhances the efficacy of PSAR signals, reducing the likelihood of whipsaws in non-trending conditions. CalculateBollingerBands(List<PseudoCandlestick>, int, double) | Edit this page View Source CalculateHistoricalSupportResistance(string, List<CandlestickData>, double, int, double, int) Calculates statistically significant historical support and resistance levels for a given market ticker. This method identifies key price levels by smoothing the frequency of price occurrences using a Gaussian filter, detecting peaks in the smoothed distribution, and ensuring a minimum distance between levels to avoid clustering. The strength of each level is calculated by combining the smoothed frequency with the total trading volume around the peak price (±1 cent). It uses the average of AskClose and BidClose prices for normalization. Price levels must have a minimum number of touches within ±1 cent of the peak, defined as a percentage of total valid candlesticks. Candlesticks with UTC timestamps between 07:00:00 and 11:59:00 (truncated to minutes) are excluded from the analysis. Returns Type Description Task<decimal> A task that represents the asynchronous operation, containing the OBV value (can be positive or negative based on net volume flow). Remarks Asynchronous execution using Task.Run for performance in high-frequency scenarios. CalculateOBV(List<PseudoCandlestick>) | Edit this page View Source CalculatePSAR(List<PseudoCandlestick>) Calculates the Parabolic Stop and Reverse (PSAR) value for a series of pseudo-candlesticks in the Kalshi marketplace. This indicator is valuable for event-driven contracts where price trends can emerge rapidly due to evolving news or data releases influencing outcome probabilities. It identifies trend directions and provides dynamic trailing stop levels, aiding risk management in volatile, bounded-price environments (1 to 99 cents per contract). However, in ranging or sideways markets, common when events are distant or uncertain, PSAR may generate frequent false reversal signals (whipsaws), potentially leading to unnecessary trades. It performs best when combined with trend-confirming indicators like the Average Directional Index (ADX) to filter signals. Declaration public double? CalculatePSAR(List<PseudoCandlestick> pseudoCandles) Parameters Type Name Description List<PseudoCandlestick> pseudoCandles The list of pseudo-candlesticks containing mid-high, mid-low, and mid-close prices. Returns Type Description double? The current PSAR value, or null if insufficient data or invalid computation. Remarks Input validation includes null check for the pseudoCandles list and parameter validation with exceptions. Parameters are sourced from configuration where applicable (e.g., PSAR acceleration factors). Different PSAR values indicate the following in Kalshi's context: A PSAR value below the current price (e.g., PSAR at 45 when the mid-close is 60) suggests an uptrend, signaling potential buying opportunities or holding long ("Yes") positions, with the PSAR serving as a rising trailing stop. A PSAR value above the current price (e.g., PSAR at 75 when the mid-close is 60) indicates a downtrend, implying selling pressure or short ("No") positions, with the PSAR acting as a falling trailing stop. Crossovers, where the price moves beyond the PSAR, denote potential trend reversals: a price rising above PSAR flips to bullish, while falling below flips to bearish. Acceleration factor (AF) increases (up to the maximum, typically 0.20) amplify the PSAR's sensitivity in strong trends, tightening stops to lock in gains. CalculatePSARAsync(List<PseudoCandlestick>) | Edit this page View Source CalculatePSARAsync(List<PseudoCandlestick>) Calculates the Parabolic Stop and Reverse (PSAR) value asynchronously for a series of pseudo-candlesticks in the Kalshi marketplace. This indicator is valuable for event-driven contracts where price trends can emerge rapidly due to evolving news or data releases influencing outcome probabilities. It identifies trend directions and provides dynamic trailing stop levels, aiding risk management in volatile, bounded-price environments (1 to 99 cents per contract). However, in ranging or sideways markets common when events are distant or uncertain, PSAR may generate frequent false reversal signals (whipsaws), potentially leading to unnecessary trades. It performs best when combined with trend-confirming indicators like the Average Directional Index (ADX) to filter signals. api/BacklashBot.Services.WebSocketMonito.title: Class WebSocketMonitorService | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.WebSocketMonito.summary: Class WebSocketMonitorService Service responsible for monitoring the Kalshi exchange status and managing WebSocket connection lifecycle. This service periodically checks the exchange's operational status and automatically connects/disconnects the WebSocket client based on exchange availability and bot initialization state. It ensures reliable real-time data streaming while handling connection failures and exchange downtime gracefully. api/BacklashBot.Services.html.title: Namespace BacklashBot.Services | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.Services.html.summary: Namespace BacklashBot.Services Classes BroadcastService Service responsible for broadcasting system status and health information to connected SignalR clients. Manages periodic check-in broadcasts at configurable intervals containing brain instance status, market data, performance metrics, and system health indicators to keep monitoring systems and dashboards updated. Includes retry logic for failed SignalR sends and performance metrics collection. CandlestickService Service responsible for managing candlestick data for trading markets. Handles loading, processing, and persistence of historical price data from both Parquet files and SQL database, including forward filling and deduplication. Provides market data population and candlestick synchronization functionality. HealthCheckService Implementation of IHealthCheckService to verify dependent services are operational. KalshiBotScopeManagerService Service for managing dependency injection scopes in the Kalshi trading bot system. This service handles the creation, initialization, and disposal of service scopes, ensuring proper resource management and access to scoped services throughout the application. Includes configurable performance metrics for monitoring scope efficiency. MarketDataInitializer Service responsible for initializing market data during application startup. This service fetches watched markets, subscribes to WebSocket channels for new markets, synchronizes market data, and sets up positions and account balance. It ensures that all necessary market data is available before the application becomes fully operational. MarketDataService Core service responsible for managing market data operations, WebSocket event handling, market watchlist management, and real-time data synchronization for the Kalshi trading bot. Supports configurable timeouts and batch sizes via MarketDataConfig, data validation for WebSocket messages, and retry logic with Polly for resilient API calls. MarketRefreshService Service responsible for managing periodic market data refresh operations for watched markets. This service runs a background task that checks trading status, determines which markets need data synchronization, and performs refresh operations at configured intervals. It provides metrics on refresh performance and supports immediate refresh triggers for specific markets. OrderBookService Service responsible for managing order book data for Kalshi markets. Handles WebSocket order book events, processes snapshots and deltas asynchronously, maintains thread-safe access to order book data, and provides synchronization capabilities. Integrates with the broader trading bot ecosystem to ensure real-time order book updates. OrderbookChangeTracker Tracks and analyzes orderbook changes for a specific market in the Kalshi trading system. This class is responsible for processing orderbook snapshots, recording individual changes, matching trades to orderbook changes, calculating various market metrics (velocity, volume, rates), and maintaining a rolling window of orderbook events for analysis. It implements the IOrderbookChangeTracker interface and integrates with the broader trading bot ecosystem to provide real-time market data analysis and metrics calculation. OverseerClientService Manages the client-side connection to an Overseer server for monitoring and oversight of the trading bot. This service establishes and maintains a SignalR connection to an Overseer instance, handles periodic check-ins with system status information, and automatically discovers and switches to better overseer servers when available. It provides resilience through automatic reconnection, connection validation, and failover mechanisms. The service integrates with the broader bot ecosystem to report market data, error counts, and performance metrics. ServiceFactory Provides centralized access to various services within the Kalshi trading bot system through dependency injection scopes. This factory implements the service locator pattern to manage service initialization, configuration validation, and thread-safe access to scoped services. It acts as a bridge between the application's dependency injection container and service consumers, ensuring proper scope management and resource cleanup. Services are cached per scope to improve performance by avoiding repeated dependency injection resolution. TradingCalculator Provides calculations for various technical indicators used in trading strategies for the Kalshi marketplace. This class implements the ITradingCalculator interface and computes indicators such as RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic Oscillator, ATR, VWAP, OBV, support/resistance levels, PSAR, and ADX based on pseudo-candlestick data. All calculations are designed for event-driven contracts with prices bounded between 1 and 99 cents. Depends on CalculationConfig for indicator parameters, includes async methods for performance in high-frequency scenarios, and incorporates comprehensive input validation including null checks, empty list handling, and data integrity checks with warnings. WebSocketMonitorService Service responsible for monitoring the Kalshi exchange status and managing WebSocket connection lifecycle. This service periodically checks the exchange's operational status and automatically connects/disconnects the WebSocket client based on exchange availability and bot initialization state. It ensures reliable real-time data streaming while handling connection failures and exchange downtime gracefully. WebSocketMonitorServiceConfig Configuration options for the WebSocketMonitorService behavior, including monitoring intervals and performance metrics settings. This configuration is loaded from the "Websocket:WebSocketMonitor" section of the application configuration.

api/BacklashBot.State.Interfaces.html.title: Namespace BacklashBot.State.Interfaces | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.State.Interfaces.html.summary: Namespace BacklashBot.State.Interfaces Interfaces IBotReadyStatus Defines the contract for tracking the readiness status of the trading bot, including initialization completion and browser readiness states. IDataCache Defines the contract for a data cache that stores and manages trading-related data including market information, account details, and system status. IMarketData Defines the comprehensive contract for market data representation, containing all trading-related information, metrics, and analysis data for a specific market. This interface provides access to real-time and historical market data, positions, technical indicators, and trading metrics. IStatusTrackerService Defines the contract for a service that tracks and manages cancellation tokens and operation status across the trading bot system.

api/BacklashBot.State.KalshiBotReadyStat.title: Class KalshiBotReadyStatus | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.State.KalshiBotReadyStat.summary: Class KalshiBotReadyStatus Manages the readiness status of different components within the Kalshi trading bot system. This class provides TaskCompletionSource objects to signal when various parts of the bot have completed their initialization or are ready for operation. Inheritance object KalshiBotReadyStatus Implements IBotReadyStatus Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.State Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class KalshiBotReadyStatus : IBotReadyStatus Remarks The readiness status system allows different components to signal their completion state: InitializationCompleted: Signals when the bot's core initialization is finished BrowserReady: Signals when browser-related components are ready (if applicable) Components can await these TaskCompletionSource objects to coordinate startup sequences, and can set the results to signal completion to waiting components. This class includes logging for state changes, input validation, and metrics collection for better debugging and monitoring. Constructors | Edit this page View Source KalshiBotReadyStatus(ILogger<KalshiBotReadyStatus>, IPerformanceMonitor, IOptions<KalshiBotReadyStatusConfig>) Initializes a new instance of the KalshiBotReadyStatus class. Creates the initial TaskCompletionSource objects and sets up the default state. Declaration public KalshiBotReadyStatus(ILogger<KalshiBotReadyStatus> logger, IPerformanceMonitor performanceMonitor, IOptions<KalshiBotReadyStatusConfig> config) Parameters Type Name Description ILogger<KalshiBotReadyStatus> logger The logger for tracking state changes and operations. IPerformanceMonitor performanceMonitor The performance monitor for recording metrics. IOptions<KalshiBotReadyStatusConfig> config Configuration options for the KalshiBotReadyStatus. Properties | Edit this page View Source BrowserReady Gets or sets the TaskCompletionSource that signals when browser-related components are ready. This is used for components that depend on browser initialization or UI readiness. Declaration public TaskCompletionSource<bool> BrowserReady { get; set; } Property Value Type Description TaskCompletionSource<bool> The TaskCompletionSource for browser readiness signaling. | Edit this page View Source InitializationCompleted Gets or sets the TaskCompletionSource that signals when the bot's core initialization is complete. Components can await this task to wait for initialization to finish, or set its result to signal completion. Declaration public TaskCompletionSource<bool> InitializationCompleted { get; set; } Property Value Type Description TaskCompletionSource<bool> The TaskCompletionSource for initialization completion signaling. Methods | Edit this page View Source ResetAll() Resets all readiness status indicators to their initial state. This creates new TaskCompletionSource objects and sets the initialization status to false. Declaration public void ResetAll() Remarks This method is typically called when restarting the bot or when a reset is needed to clear all previous readiness signals and start fresh. | Edit this page View Source SetBrowserReady(bool) Sets the browser ready status with validation and logging. Declaration public void SetBrowserReady(bool result) Parameters Type Name Description bool result The result to set for browser readiness. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException Thrown when TaskCompletionSource is null. InvalidOperationException Thrown when trying to set result on already completed task. | Edit this page View Source SetInitializationCompleted(bool) Sets the initialization completion status with validation and logging. Declaration public void SetInitializationCompleted(bool result) Parameters Type Name Description bool result The result to set for initialization completion. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException Thrown when TaskCompletionSource is null. InvalidOperationException Thrown when trying to set result on already completed task. Implements IBotReadyStatus

api/BacklashBot.State.KalshiBotStatusTra.title: Enum KalshiBotStatusTracker.BrainMode | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.State.KalshiBotStatusTra.summary: Enum KalshiBotStatusTracker.BrainMode Enum representing the brain mode of the bot. Namespace: BacklashBot.State Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public enum KalshiBotStatusTracker.BrainMode Fields Name Description Autonomous Autonomous mode: bot operates independently and subscribes to all channels. Servant Servant mode: bot is controlled by an overseer and skips non-market-specific channels.

api/BacklashBot.State.KalshiBotStatusTra.title: Class KalshiBotStatusTracker | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.State.KalshiBotStatusTra.summary: Class KalshiBotStatusTracker Manages the global cancellation state for the Kalshi trading bot system. This class provides a centralized CancellationTokenSource that can be used to coordinate graceful shutdown and cancellation across all components of the bot. Inheritance object KalshiBotStatusTracker Implements IStatusTrackerService IDisposable IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashBot.State Assembly: BacklashBot.dll Syntax public class KalshiBotStatusTracker : IStatusTrackerService, IDisposable, IAsyncDisposable Remarks The status tracker ensures thread-safe access to the global cancellation token and provides: A single CancellationToken that all components can monitor for shutdown signals Thread-safe cancellation operations Proper resource cleanup through IDisposable and IAsyncDisposable implementation Reset capability for restarting the cancellation state Logging for cancellation operations and metrics collection This is registered as a singleton service to ensure all components share the same cancellation state. Constructors | Edit this page View Source KalshiBotStatusTracker(ILogger<KalshiBotStatusTracker>, IPerformanceMonitor?, IOptions<KalshiBotStatusTrackerConfig>) Initializes a new instance of the KalshiBotStatusTracker class. Creates the initial CancellationTokenSource and sets up the cancellation state. Declaration public KalshiBotStatusTracker(ILogger<KalshiBotStatusTracker> logger, IPerformanceMonitor? performanceMonitor, IOptions<KalshiBotStatusTrackerConfig> config) Parameters Type Name Description ILogger<KalshiBotStatusTracker> logger The logger for tracking cancellation operations. IPerformanceMonitor performanceMonitor The performance monitor for recording metrics. IOptions<KalshiBotStatusTrackerConfig> config Configuration options for the KalshiBotStatusTracker. api/BacklashBot.State.MarketData.html.title: Class MarketData | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.State.MarketData.html.summary: Class MarketData Represents the comprehensive data model for a specific Kalshi market, aggregating real-time and historical market data from WebSocket feeds, API responses, and calculated metrics. This class serves as the central hub for all market-related information used in trading decisions, technical analysis, and snapshot creation. The MarketData class maintains: Current order book state with bid/ask prices and depths Historical candlestick data across multiple timeframes (minute, hour, day) Real-time ticker updates and price movements Position information and PandL calculations Technical indicators (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, etc.) Order book change velocities and trade metrics Support/resistance levels and market metadata Data integrity is ensured through comprehensive input validation: Candlestick data validation for non-negative prices (0-100), valid timestamps, and reasonable values Ticker data validation for price ranges, volume, and timestamp integrity Orderbook data validation for price bounds, quantities, and side validity Position data validation for size reasonableness, exposure bounds, and update timestamps Invalid data triggers informative warnings via logging but processing continues to maintain system resilience. Data is updated through various mechanisms: WebSocket events for real-time order book and ticker updates Periodic API calls for position and market information Asynchronous background calculations for technical indicators and metrics to avoid blocking Historical data loading from candlestick storage Performance optimizations include: Efficient data structures and minimized LINQ queries Parallel processing for CPU-intensive calculations Binary search for large dataset lookups Asynchronous operations to maintain responsiveness Calculation parameters such as tolerance percentages, fee rates, time periods for slope calculations, and lookback periods are configurable via CalculationConfig to allow runtime customization without code changes. This class implements the IMarketData interface and is used throughout the trading bot for market analysis, strategy execution, and data persistence via MarketSnapshot serialization. Sets up all data collections, dependencies, and initial state for market data tracking. Initializes candlestick dictionaries for minute, hour, and day timeframes, and configures calculation parameters from the provided configuration. api/BacklashBot.State.html.title: Namespace BacklashBot.State | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashBot.State.html.summary: Namespace BacklashBot.State Classes DataCache Provides a centralized cache for market data, account information, and trading status. Implements thread-safe storage for real-time trading data and state management. KalshiBotReadyStatus Manages the readiness status of different components within the Kalshi trading bot system. This class provides TaskCompletionSource objects to signal when various parts of the bot have completed their initialization or are ready for operation. KalshiBotStatusTracker Manages the global cancellation state for the Kalshi trading bot system. This class provides a centralized CancellationTokenSource that can be used to coordinate graceful shutdown and cancellation across all components of the bot. MarketData Represents the comprehensive data model for a specific Kalshi market, aggregating real-time and historical market data from WebSocket feeds, API responses, and calculated metrics. This class serves as the central hub for all market-related information used in trading decisions, technical analysis, and snapshot creation. The MarketData class maintains: Current order book state with bid/ask prices and depths Historical candlestick data across multiple timeframes (minute, hour, day) Real-time ticker updates and price movements Position information and PandL calculations Technical indicators (RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands, etc.) Order book change velocities and trade metrics Support/resistance levels and market metadata Data integrity is ensured through comprehensive input validation: Candlestick data validation for non-negative prices (0-100), valid timestamps, and reasonable values Ticker data validation for price ranges, volume, and timestamp integrity Orderbook data validation for price bounds, quantities, and side validity Position data validation for size reasonableness, exposure bounds, and update timestamps Invalid data triggers informative warnings via logging but processing continues to maintain system resilience. Data is updated through various mechanisms: WebSocket events for real-time order book and ticker updates Periodic API calls for position and market information Asynchronous background calculations for technical indicators and metrics to avoid blocking Historical data loading from candlestick storage Performance optimizations include: Efficient data structures and minimized LINQ queries Parallel processing for CPU-intensive calculations Binary search for large dataset lookups Asynchronous operations to maintain responsiveness Calculation parameters such as tolerance percentages, fee rates, time periods for slope calculations, and lookback periods are configurable via CalculationConfig to allow runtime customization without code changes. This class implements the IMarketData interface and is used throughout the trading bot for market analysis, strategy execution, and data persistence via MarketSnapshot serialization. Enums KalshiBotStatusTracker.BrainMode Enum representing the brain mode of the bot.

api/BacklashDTOs.Exceptions.html.title: Namespace BacklashDTOs.Exceptions | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashDTOs.Exceptions.html.summary: Namespace BacklashDTOs.Exceptions Classes CandlestickFetchException Exception thrown when there is an error fetching candlestick data. CandlestickTransientFailureException Exception thrown when there is a transient failure in candlestick data. ConnectionDisruptionException Exception thrown when there is a connection disruption. DeadLockException Exception thrown when a deadlock occurs. KalshiKeyFileNotFoundException Exception thrown when the Kalshi key file is not found. KnownDuplicateInsertException Exception thrown when a known duplicate insert occurs. MarketInterestScoreDeadlockException Exception thrown when a deadlock occurs in market interest score. MarketInvalidException Exception thrown when market data is invalid. MarketTransientFailureException Exception thrown when there is a transient failure in market data. NotInCacheException Exception thrown when data is not found in cache. OrderbookTransientFailureException Exception thrown when there is a transient failure in the orderbook. ProcessingThresholdExceededException Exception thrown when processing threshold is exceeded. SnapshotInvalidException Exception thrown when snapshot data is invalid. TradeMissedException Exception thrown when a trade is missed. UnhandledSmokehouseException Exception for unhandled Smokehouse errors. WebSocketRetryFailedException Exception thrown when WebSocket retry fails. Interfaces IBacklashBotException Interface for BacklashBot exceptions.

api/BacklashDTOs.KalshiAPI.html.title: Namespace BacklashDTOs.KalshiAPI | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashDTOs.KalshiAPI.html.summary: Namespace BacklashDTOs.KalshiAPI Classes APICandlestick Represents a single candlestick data point for a Kalshi market, including price and volume info. AnnouncementApi Represents an announcement from the Kalshi API. AnnouncementResponse Represents the response containing a list of announcements from the Kalshi API. ApiError Represents an error response from the Kalshi API. BalanceResponse Represents a user's account balance response from the Kalshi API. BatchOrderResponseItem Represents an item in the batch order response from the Kalshi API. BatchOrdersRequest Represents a request to create multiple orders in batch from the Kalshi API. BatchOrdersResponse Represents the response for batch orders from the Kalshi API. CancelOrderResponse Represents the response for canceling an order from the Kalshi API. CandlestickResponse Represents a response containing candlestick data for a specific market on Kalshi. CreateOrderRequest Represents a request to create an order on the Kalshi platform. CreateOrderResponse Represents the response for creating an order from the Kalshi API. DeleteBatchOrderResponseItem Represents an item in the delete batch order response from the Kalshi API. DeleteOrdersBatchRequest Represents a request to delete multiple orders in batch from the Kalshi API. DeleteOrdersBatchResponse Represents the response for deleting multiple orders in batch from the Kalshi API. EventMetadataResponse Represents the response containing event metadata from the Kalshi API. EventPosition Represents a user's aggregated position for an event across multiple markets on Kalshi. EventResponse Represents the response containing an event from the Kalshi API. EventsResponse Represents the response containing a list of events from the Kalshi API. ExchangeSchedule Represents the exchange schedule from the Kalshi API. ExchangeScheduleResponse Represents the response containing the exchange schedule from the Kalshi API. ExchangeStatus Represents the operational status of the Kalshi exchange and trading system. Fill Represents a fill (trade execution) from the Kalshi API. FillsResponse Represents the response containing a list of fills from the Kalshi API. IncentiveProgram Represents an incentive program from the Kalshi API. IncentiveProgramsResponse Represents the response containing a list of incentive programs from the Kalshi API. KalshiEvent Represents a Kalshi event. KalshiMarket Represents a market on the Kalshi platform with details such as ticker, prices, and trading status. KalshiMilestone Represents a milestone from the Kalshi API. KalshiSeries Represents a Kalshi series. MaintenanceWindowApi Represents a maintenance window. MarketPositionApi Represents a user's position in a specific Kalshi market, including trading stats and profit/loss. MarketResponse Represents a response containing a list of Kalshi markets and pagination info. MilestonesResponse Represents the response containing a list of milestones from the Kalshi API. OrderApi Represents an order placed on the Kalshi platform with details about its status and execution. OrderDetails Represents the details of an order from the Kalshi API. OrderQueuePositionResponse Represents the response containing the queue position of an order from the Kalshi API. OrderResponse Represents the response containing an order from the Kalshi API. OrderbookMessage Represents a message containing order book data, either as a snapshot or delta update. OrdersResponse Represents the response containing a list of orders from the Kalshi API. PositionsResponse Represents a response containing market and event position data. PriceData Represents price-related data for a market or event. PriceLevel Represents a price level in the order book with price and resting contract details. QueuePositionItem Represents an item containing queue position information for an order. QueuePositionsResponse Represents the response containing a list of queue positions from the Kalshi API. ScheduleData Represents the schedule data. SeriesResponse Represents the response containing a series from the Kalshi API. Settlement Represents a settlement from the Kalshi API. SettlementSource Represents a settlement source from the Kalshi API. SettlementsResponse Represents the response containing a list of settlements from the Kalshi API. StandardHours Represents the standard hours for trading. StandardHoursApi Represents the standard hours for trading. TotalRestingOrderValueResponse Represents the response containing the total resting order value from the Kalshi API. TradingPeriod Represents a trading period with open and close times. TradingSession Represents a trading session.

api/BacklashDTOs.MarketSnapshot.html.title: Class MarketSnapshot | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashDTOs.MarketSnapshot.html.summary: Class MarketSnapshot Represents a complete snapshot of market data at a specific point in time, used for historical record storage and trading strategy analysis. This DTO captures all relevant market state including order book, positions, technical indicators, and calculated metrics. MarketSnapshot is created from MarketData instances and serialized for persistence. It includes: Market identification and metadata (ticker, category, status) Current order book state with bid/ask prices and depths Position information and P&L calculations Technical indicators and trading metrics Historical price data and support/resistance levels Calculated velocities, spreads, and market statistics The snapshot schema evolves over time, with version tracking to ensure backward compatibility during upgrades and data migration. Declaration public double ExpectedFees { get; } Property Value Type Description double Remarks Calculated using the formula: fees = roundup(0.07 * C * P * (1-P)), where C is the number of contracts (absolute position size), P is the liquidation price in dollars, and roundup rounds to the next cent. Fees are summed across price levels in the order book when available, or based on the current bid (for "yes" positions) or ask (for "no" positions) price if no orders exist. Only applied for takers, not makers. Sourced from MarketData.RefreshPositionMetadata. api/BacklashDTOs.html.title: Namespace BacklashDTOs | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashDTOs.html.summary: Namespace BacklashDTOs Classes BollingerBandsConverter JSON converter for Bollinger Bands tuples. BrainPerformanceMetricsDTO Represents performance metrics. CacheSnapshot Represents a snapshot of the trading system's state at a specific point in time. CandleMids Helper class to cache candlestick mid-price values. CandlestickData Represents candlestick data for a market. ErrorHandlerTaskInfo Represents information about an error handler task. EventLifecycleEventArgs Event arguments for event lifecycle events. FillEventArgs Event arguments for fill events. MACDConverter JSON converter for MACD tuples. MarketLifecycleEventArgs Event arguments for market lifecycle events. MarketLiquidityStatsDTO Represents market liquidity statistics. MarketSnapshot Represents a complete snapshot of market data at a specific point in time, used for historical record storage and trading strategy analysis. This DTO captures all relevant market state including order book, positions, technical indicators, and calculated metrics. MarketSnapshot is created from MarketData instances and serialized for persistence. It includes: Market identification and metadata (ticker, category, status) Current order book state with bid/ask prices and depths Position information and P&L calculations Technical indicators and trading metrics Historical price data and support/resistance levels Calculated velocities, spreads, and market statistics The snapshot schema evolves over time, with version tracking to ensure backward compatibility during upgrades and data migration. MarketSnapshot.Difference Represents a difference between two MarketSnapshot instances. MarketSnapshotDelta Represents the delta between two MarketSnapshot instances, tracking which properties have changed. OrderBookEventArgs Event arguments for order book events. OrderbookChange Represents a change in the orderbook. OrderbookData Represents orderbook data for a specific market, focused on orderbook entries. PseudoCandlestick Represents a pseudo candlestick data point. SignalRClient Represents a SignalR client. SimplifiedTupleConverter A JSON converter factory for simplified tuple types used in the application. SimulatedOrderbook Represents a simulated orderbook for trading operations. SimulatedOrderbook.OrderLevel Represents an order level in the order book. StatusChangedEventArgs Provides data for the status changed event. StochasticOscillatorConverter JSON converter for Stochastic Oscillator tuples. SupportResistanceLevel Represents a support or resistance level in trading. TickerEventArgs Event arguments for ticker events. TradeEvent Represents a trade event in the market. TradeEventArgs Event arguments for trade events.

api/BacklashInterfaces.Constants.KalshiC.summary: Enum KalshiConstants.MarketStatus Enum representing the possible statuses of a market. Namespace: BacklashInterfaces.Constants Assembly: BacklashInterfaces.dll Syntax public enum KalshiConstants.MarketStatus Fields Name Description Active Market is actively trading and accepting orders. Bad Market has been marked as invalid or problematic. Closed Market has been closed and no longer accepts orders. Determined Market outcome has been determined. Finalized Market has been finalized and trading is complete. Inactive Market is inactive and not available for trading. Initialized Market has been initialized but not yet opened. Open Market is open for trading but not yet active. Settled Market has been settled and payouts distributed. Unopened Market has not yet opened for trading.
