api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.title: Enum StrategyEnums.ActionType | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.summary: Enum StrategyEnums.ActionType Defines different types of trading actions that can be taken in response to market conditions. Namespace: BacklashInterfaces.Enums Assembly: BacklashInterfaces.dll Syntax public enum StrategyEnums.ActionType Fields Name Description Cancel Indicates the action to cancel any resting orders in the market. Exit Indicates the action to exit an existing trading position completely. Long Indicates the action to enter a long position by buying the asset. LongPostAsk Indicates a market long position followed by immediately posting a resting sell order for the purchased amount. None Indicates that no trading action should be taken at this time. PostAsk Indicates the action to place a limit sell order for the 'yes' outcome. PostYes Indicates the action to place a limit buy order for the 'yes' outcome. Short Indicates the action to enter a short position by selling the asset. ShortPostYes Indicates a market short position followed by immediately posting a resting buy order for the sold amount.

api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.title: Enum StrategyEnums.ComparisonOperator | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.summary: Enum StrategyEnums.ComparisonOperator Defines different types of comparison operators used in trading strategy conditions. Namespace: BacklashInterfaces.Enums Assembly: BacklashInterfaces.dll Syntax public enum StrategyEnums.ComparisonOperator Fields Name Description EqualTo Represents a condition where a value is equal to a specified threshold. Exists Represents a condition where a value exists or is present. GreaterThan Represents a condition where a value is greater than a specified threshold. GreaterThanOrEqual Represents a condition where a value is greater than or equal to a specified threshold. LessThan Represents a condition where a value is less than a specified threshold. LessThanOrEqual Represents a condition where a value is less than or equal to a specified threshold. NotExists Represents a condition where a value does not exist or is absent.

api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.title: Enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_ActivityLevel | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.summary: Enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_ActivityLevel Defines different levels of market activity conditions for trading strategies. Namespace: BacklashInterfaces.Enums Assembly: BacklashInterfaces.dll Syntax public enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_ActivityLevel Fields Name Description High Indicates high trading activity, characterized by high volume or frequent trades. Low Indicates low trading activity, characterized by low volume or infrequent trades. Moderate Indicates moderate trading activity, neither particularly high nor low in volume or frequency.

api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.title: Enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_ClosingTime | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.summary: Enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_ClosingTime Defines different time-based conditions relative to market closing for trading strategies. Namespace: BacklashInterfaces.Enums Assembly: BacklashInterfaces.dll Syntax public enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_ClosingTime Fields Name Description Far Indicates that a long time remains until market expiry, such as weeks or months. Imminent Indicates that the market is very close to expiry, such as minutes or hours remaining. Near Indicates that the market is close to expiry, such as days or hours remaining.

api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.title: Enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_Liquidity | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.summary: Enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_Liquidity Defines different levels of market liquidity conditions for trading strategies. Namespace: BacklashInterfaces.Enums Assembly: BacklashInterfaces.dll Syntax public enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_Liquidity Fields Name Description High Indicates high liquidity, making it easy to trade large volumes without significantly affecting prices. Low Indicates low liquidity, making it difficult to trade without impacting market prices. Moderate Indicates moderate liquidity, providing average trading conditions neither particularly high nor low.

api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.title: Enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_MarketCategory | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.summary: Enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_MarketCategory Defines different categories of markets for trading strategies. Namespace: BacklashInterfaces.Enums Assembly: BacklashInterfaces.dll Syntax public enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_MarketCategory Fields Name Description COVID19 Represents markets related to COVID-19 and pandemic conditions and impacts. ClimateAndWeather Represents markets related to climate and weather conditions and predictions. Companies Represents markets related to companies and corporate events or performance. Crypto Represents markets related to cryptocurrency and blockchain technology. Economics Represents markets related to economic indicators and macroeconomic conditions. Education Represents markets related to education and academic topics or events. Elections Represents markets related to elections and political voting outcomes. Entertainment Represents markets related to entertainment and media industry events. Financials Represents markets related to financial markets and investment instruments. Health Represents markets related to health and medical topics or advancements. Mentions Represents markets related to mentions and social media buzz or trends. Politics Represents markets related to politics and government actions or policies. ScienceAndTechnology Represents markets related to science and technology innovations or discoveries. Social Represents markets related to social issues and cultural topics or events. Sports Represents markets related to sports and athletic competitions or events. Transportation Represents markets related to transportation and travel industry conditions. World Represents markets related to world events and global topics or occurrences.

api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.title: Enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_PriceMovement | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.summary: Enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_PriceMovement Defines different types of price movement conditions for trading strategies. Namespace: BacklashInterfaces.Enums Assembly: BacklashInterfaces.dll Syntax public enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_PriceMovement Fields Name Description Bouncing Indicates that the price is oscillating within a defined range without establishing a clear trend. Stable Indicates that the price is relatively steady with minimal movement or changes. Trending Indicates that the price is consistently moving in one direction, either upward or downward. Volatile Indicates that the price is experiencing significant and rapid fluctuations.

api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.title: Enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_UncertaintySignal | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.summary: Enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_UncertaintySignal Defines different levels of market uncertainty conditions for trading strategies. Namespace: BacklashInterfaces.Enums Assembly: BacklashInterfaces.dll Syntax public enum StrategyEnums.Conditions_UncertaintySignal Fields Name Description High Indicates that the market outcome is unclear or highly uncertain. Low Indicates that the market outcome is relatively clear or certain.

api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.title: Enum StrategyEnums.MarketType | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.StrategyEnu.summary: Enum StrategyEnums.MarketType Defines different types of market conditions and behaviors for trading strategies. Namespace: BacklashInterfaces.Enums Assembly: BacklashInterfaces.dll Syntax public enum StrategyEnums.MarketType Fields Name Description Bouncing Represents a market with prices oscillating within a defined range, making it suitable for mean-reversion trading strategies. EventDriven Represents an event-specific market such as awards, launches, or one-time occurrences. FarStable Represents a long-term stable market with predictable behavior over extended periods. FinancialMomentum Represents a momentum-driven market in financial assets and instruments. HighUncertainty Represents a market with high uncertainty, often characterized by balanced probabilities or unclear outcomes. ImminentCloseVolatile Represents a market close to resolution with high volatility due to impending settlement. LowLiquidity Represents a market with low liquidity, where trading large volumes can significantly impact prices due to limited market depth. NewsDriven Represents a market primarily driven by news events and market sentiment. SeasonalVolatile Represents a volatile market with seasonal or predictable patterns in price movement. StableMacro Represents a stable market influenced by macroeconomic factors and indicators. Stagnant Represents a market with minimal price movement, low trading activity, and stable conditions. TechTrend Represents a trending market in technology or innovation-related sectors. Trending Represents a market with consistent price movement in one direction, ideal for momentum-based trading strategies. TrendingActive Represents a trending market with high levels of trading activity and volume. Undefined Represents an undefined or unmapped market condition, used as a default value when the market type cannot be determined. Volatile Represents a market experiencing significant price fluctuations and high volatility. VolatileEvents Represents a volatile market driven by specific events or news catalysts.

api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.Subscriptio.title: Enum SubscriptionState | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.Subscriptio.summary: Enum SubscriptionState Represents the current state of a subscription to a specific market data channel via WebSocket. This enum is used by the SubscriptionManager to track and manage the lifecycle of subscriptions, ensuring proper handling of connection states and preventing duplicate or invalid operations. Namespace: BacklashInterfaces.Enums Assembly: BacklashInterfaces.dll Syntax public enum SubscriptionState Fields Name Description Subscribed The subscription is active and confirmed. Data for the channel is being received and processed. This state indicates a successful connection and ongoing data flow. Subscribing A subscription request has been sent to the server, but confirmation has not yet been received. This transitional state prevents multiple concurrent subscription attempts for the same channel. Unsubscribed The subscription is not active. This is the initial state before any subscription attempt, or after a successful unsubscription. In this state, no data is being received for the channel. Unsubscribing An unsubscription request has been sent to the server, but confirmation has not yet been received. This transitional state ensures clean disconnection and prevents new subscription attempts during teardown.

api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.html.title: Namespace BacklashInterfaces.Enums | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashInterfaces.Enums.html.summary: Namespace BacklashInterfaces.Enums Classes StrategyEnums Contains enumeration types used throughout the trading strategy system for defining market conditions, actions, and various trading parameters. Enums StrategyEnums.ActionType Defines different types of trading actions that can be taken in response to market conditions. StrategyEnums.ComparisonOperator Defines different types of comparison operators used in trading strategy conditions. StrategyEnums.Conditions_ActivityLevel Defines different levels of market activity conditions for trading strategies. StrategyEnums.Conditions_ClosingTime Defines different time-based conditions relative to market closing for trading strategies. StrategyEnums.Conditions_Liquidity Defines different levels of market liquidity conditions for trading strategies. StrategyEnums.Conditions_MarketCategory Defines different categories of markets for trading strategies. StrategyEnums.Conditions_PriceMovement Defines different types of price movement conditions for trading strategies. StrategyEnums.Conditions_UncertaintySignal Defines different levels of market uncertainty conditions for trading strategies. StrategyEnums.MarketType Defines different types of market conditions and behaviors for trading strategies. SubscriptionState Represents the current state of a subscription to a specific market data channel via WebSocket. This enum is used by the SubscriptionManager to track and manage the lifecycle of subscriptions, ensuring proper handling of connection states and preventing duplicate or invalid operations.

api/BacklashInterfaces.PerformanceMetric.title: Enum VisualType | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashInterfaces.PerformanceMetric.summary: Enum VisualType Defines the visual representation type for performance metrics in GUI components. This allows uniform handling of different metric types across all performance monitors. Namespace: BacklashInterfaces.PerformanceMetrics Assembly: BacklashInterfaces.dll Syntax public enum VisualType Fields Name Description Badge Badge or notification counter (good for alerts, warnings) Counter Animated counter with rolling numbers (good for counts, totals) DataGrid Data grid or table (good for detailed breakdowns) DisabledMetric Disabled metric indicator (for metrics that are not active) LineChart Time-series line chart (good for trends over time) NumericDisplay Simple numeric display with optional formatting PieChart Pie or donut chart (good for proportions, cache hit rates) ProgressBar Linear progress bar (good for completion ratios, resource usage) SpeedDial Speed dial or circular gauge (good for time durations, percentages) TrafficLight Traffic light indicator (good for threshold-based metrics)

api/BacklashOverseer.Models.html.title: Namespace BacklashOverseer.Models | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashOverseer.Models.html.summary: Namespace BacklashOverseer.Models Classes BrainPersistence Represents the persistent state of a brain instance in the Kalshi trading bot overseer system. This class encapsulates all the configuration, status, and historical performance data needed to maintain and restore a brain's operational state across application restarts. BrainPersistenceDefaults Configuration class for default values used in BrainPersistence initialization. Provides centralized configuration options that can be loaded from appsettings.json. MetricHistory Represents a single historical data point for a performance metric. Used to track time-series data for various system performance indicators.

api/BacklashOverseer.Overseer.html.title: Class Overseer | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashOverseer.Overseer.html.summary: Class Overseer Central orchestrator for the KalshiBot Overseer system that manages WebSocket connections, handles real-time market data events, coordinates periodic data fetching, and maintains system health monitoring. This class serves as the main entry point for overseer operations, integrating with WebSocket clients, database services, and SignalR hubs to provide comprehensive monitoring and control of the trading bot ecosystem. Key features: Configurable API fetch intervals (default: 10 minutes) Configurable system info logging intervals (default: 1 minute) Performance metrics collection for WebSocket events and API operations Input validation for event data processing to prevent null reference exceptions Progress reporting for long-running API fetch operations Batch processing for brain persistence logging (default batch size: 50) Configurable SignalR broadcast batch sizes (default: 10) Inheritance object Overseer Implements IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashOverseer Assembly: BacklashOverseer.dll Syntax public class Overseer : IDisposable Constructors | Edit this page View Source Overseer(IKalshiWebSocketClient, IServiceScopeFactory, ILogger<Overseer>, IHubContext<OverseerHub>, IOptions<OverseerConfig>, IPerformanceMonitor) Initializes a new instance of the Overseer class with required dependencies. IServiceScopeFactory scopeFactory Factory for creating service scopes for dependency injection. ILogger<Overseer> logger Logger for recording system events and diagnostics. IHubContext<OverseerHub> hubContext SignalR hub context for real-time client communication. IOptions<OverseerConfig> config Configuration options for the overseer system. api/BacklashOverseer.Services.BrainPersi.title: Class BrainPersistenceService | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashOverseer.Services.BrainPersi.summary: Class BrainPersistenceService Service responsible for managing brain instance states with database persistence. This service provides thread-safe operations for storing, retrieving, and updating brain configuration, market watch lists, and performance metrics history. It supports in-memory storage and database persistence for recovery after application restarts. The service includes configurable history retention, performance metrics collection, batch operations, and comprehensive health monitoring. api/BacklashOverseer.Services.html.title: Namespace BacklashOverseer.Services | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashOverseer.Services.html.summary: Namespace BacklashOverseer.Services Classes BrainPersistenceService Service responsible for managing brain instance states with database persistence. This service provides thread-safe operations for storing, retrieving, and updating brain configuration, market watch lists, and performance metrics history. It supports in-memory storage and database persistence for recovery after application restarts. The service includes configurable history retention, performance metrics collection, batch operations, and comprehensive health monitoring. OverseerWebSocketMonitorService Lightweight service for monitoring WebSocket connections and exchange status. This service monitors the exchange status and manages WebSocket connections without modifying BacklashBot-specific objects. PerformanceMetricsService Centralized service for collecting and managing performance metrics across the KalshiBot Overseer system. This service aggregates metrics from various components including WebSocket operations, API calls, SignalR communications, overnight tasks, and snapshot processing. SnapshotService Provides services for retrieving and processing snapshot group data from the database. This service aggregates snapshot groups by market ticker, calculates recorded hours, market hours, and recorded hours percentages, and returns structured data for analysis.

api/BacklashOverseer.State.OverseerReady.summary: Class OverseerReadyStatus Manages the readiness status of different components within the Kalshi overseer system. This class provides TaskCompletionSource objects to signal when various parts of the overseer have completed their initialization or are ready for operation, with comprehensive logging and metrics. Inheritance object OverseerReadyStatus Implements IBotReadyStatus Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashOverseer.State Assembly: BacklashOverseer.dll Syntax public class OverseerReadyStatus : IBotReadyStatus Remarks The readiness status system allows different components to signal their completion state: InitializationCompleted: Signals when the overseer's core initialization is finished BrowserReady: Signals when browser-related components are ready (if applicable) Components can await these TaskCompletionSource objects to coordinate startup sequences, and can set the results to signal completion to waiting components. Features include: Comprehensive logging for all state changes and operations Input validation to prevent null reference exceptions Default states are hardcoded to false for initialization and browser readiness Metrics collection for readiness timing and operation performance Constructors | Edit this page View Source OverseerReadyStatus(ILogger<OverseerReadyStatus>) Initializes a new instance of the OverseerReadyStatus class. Creates the initial TaskCompletionSource objects and sets up the default state. Declaration public OverseerReadyStatus(ILogger<OverseerReadyStatus> logger) Parameters Type Name Description ILogger<OverseerReadyStatus> logger The logger for tracking readiness state changes. Properties | Edit this page View Source BrowserReady Gets or sets the TaskCompletionSource that signals when browser-related components are ready. This is used for components that depend on browser initialization or UI readiness. Declaration public TaskCompletionSource<bool> BrowserReady { get; set; } Property Value Type Description TaskCompletionSource<bool> The TaskCompletionSource for browser readiness signaling. | Edit this page View Source InitializationCompleted Gets or sets the TaskCompletionSource that signals when the overseer's core initialization is complete. Components can await this task to wait for initialization to finish, or set its result to signal completion. Declaration public TaskCompletionSource<bool> InitializationCompleted { get; set; } Property Value Type Description TaskCompletionSource<bool> The TaskCompletionSource for initialization completion signaling. Methods | Edit this page View Source ResetAll() Resets all readiness status indicators to their initial state. This creates new TaskCompletionSource objects and sets the initialization status to the configured default. api/BacklashOverseer.State.OverseerStatu.title: Class OverseerStatusTracker | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashOverseer.State.OverseerStatu.summary: Class OverseerStatusTracker Manages the global cancellation state for the Kalshi overseer system. This class provides a centralized CancellationTokenSource that can be used to coordinate graceful shutdown and cancellation across all components of the overseer. Inheritance object OverseerStatusTracker Implements IStatusTrackerService IDisposable IAsyncDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: BacklashOverseer.State Assembly: BacklashOverseer.dll Syntax public class OverseerStatusTracker : IStatusTrackerService, IDisposable, IAsyncDisposable Remarks The status tracker ensures thread-safe access to the global cancellation token and provides: A single CancellationToken that all components can monitor for shutdown signals Thread-safe cancellation operations with comprehensive logging Proper resource cleanup through IDisposable and IAsyncDisposable implementations Reset capability for restarting the cancellation state with timing metrics Metrics collection for cancellation frequency and operation timing This is registered as a singleton service to ensure all components share the same cancellation state. Constructors | Edit this page View Source OverseerStatusTracker(ILogger<OverseerStatusTracker>) Initializes a new instance of the OverseerStatusTracker class. Creates the initial CancellationTokenSource and sets up the cancellation state with logging and metrics. Declaration public OverseerStatusTracker(ILogger<OverseerStatusTracker> logger) Parameters Type Name Description ILogger<OverseerStatusTracker> logger The logger for tracking cancellation operations and state changes. Methods | Edit this page View Source CancelAll() Cancels all operations by triggering the global cancellation token. This signals all components that are monitoring the cancellation token to stop their operations. Declaration public void CancelAll() Remarks This method is thread-safe and should be called when the overseer needs to shut down. After calling this method, the cancellation token will be in a cancelled state. | Edit this page View Source Dispose() Disposes of the resources used by the status tracker synchronously. This cancels any pending operations and disposes of the CancellationTokenSource and metrics. Declaration public void Dispose() Remarks This method should be called when the status tracker is no longer needed, typically during application shutdown. It ensures proper cleanup of unmanaged resources and logs the disposal operation for monitoring purposes. | Edit this page View Source DisposeAsync() Asynchronously disposes of the resources used by the status tracker. This cancels any pending operations and disposes of the CancellationTokenSource. Declaration public ValueTask DisposeAsync() Returns Type Description ValueTask A ValueTask representing the asynchronous dispose operation. | Edit this page View Source GetCancellationToken() Gets the global CancellationToken that all components should monitor for shutdown signals. Declaration public CancellationToken GetCancellationToken() Returns Type Description CancellationToken The CancellationToken that can be used to monitor for cancellation requests. Remarks This method provides thread-safe access to the cancellation token. Components should regularly check this token or use it in async operations to respond to shutdown requests gracefully. | Edit this page View Source ResetAll() Resets the cancellation state by creating a new CancellationTokenSource. This clears any previous cancellation state and allows the overseer to restart operations. Declaration public void ResetAll() Remarks This method properly disposes of the old CancellationTokenSource before creating a new one. It should be called when restarting the overseer or when a fresh cancellation state is needed. Implements IStatusTrackerService IDisposable IAsyncDisposable

api/BacklashOverseer.html.title: Namespace BacklashOverseer | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/BacklashOverseer.html.summary: Namespace BacklashOverseer Classes Overseer Central orchestrator for the KalshiBot Overseer system that manages WebSocket connections, handles real-time market data events, coordinates periodic data fetching, and maintains system health monitoring. This class serves as the main entry point for overseer operations, integrating with WebSocket clients, database services, and SignalR hubs to provide comprehensive monitoring and control of the trading bot ecosystem. Key features: Configurable API fetch intervals (default: 10 minutes) Configurable system info logging intervals (default: 1 minute) Performance metrics collection for WebSocket events and API operations Input validation for event data processing to prevent null reference exceptions Progress reporting for long-running API fetch operations Batch processing for brain persistence logging (default batch size: 50) Configurable SignalR broadcast batch sizes (default: 10) OverseerHub SignalR hub that manages real-time communication between the Kalshi trading bot overseer and connected clients. This hub handles client connections, authentication via handshakes, periodic status check-ins from brain instances, and broadcasting of trading data and updates. It serves as the central communication point for the overseer system, enabling real-time monitoring and control of trading bot operations. OverseerHub.ClientInfo Represents information about a connected client, including identification, connection details, and last activity timestamp. Startup Configures services and the request pipeline for the BacklashOverseer application.

api/KalshiBotAPI.KalshiAPI.KalshiAPIServ.summary: Class KalshiAPIService Provides a comprehensive service for interacting with the Kalshi trading API. This service handles authentication, market data retrieval, order management, position tracking, and various other API operations required for automated trading on the Kalshi platform. It implements the IKalshiAPIService interface and uses RSA-based authentication with API keys. The service maintains execution time tracking for performance monitoring, supports parallel processing for high-volume data operations, and includes configurable parameters for lookback periods and calculations. Robust error handling with cancellation token support throughout all operations. api/KalshiBotAPI.KalshiAPI.html.title: Namespace KalshiBotAPI.KalshiAPI | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotAPI.KalshiAPI.html.summary: Namespace KalshiBotAPI.KalshiAPI Classes KalshiAPIService Provides a comprehensive service for interacting with the Kalshi trading API. This service handles authentication, market data retrieval, order management, position tracking, and various other API operations required for automated trading on the Kalshi platform. It implements the IKalshiAPIService interface and uses RSA-based authentication with API keys. The service maintains execution time tracking for performance monitoring, supports parallel processing for high-volume data operations, and includes configurable parameters for lookback periods and calculations. Robust error handling with cancellation token support throughout all operations.

api/KalshiBotAPI.WebSockets.Interfaces.h.title: Namespace KalshiBotAPI.WebSockets.Interfaces | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotAPI.WebSockets.Interfaces.h.summary: Namespace KalshiBotAPI.WebSockets.Interfaces Interfaces IKalshiWebSocketClient Defines the contract for a WebSocket client that handles real-time communication with the Kalshi trading platform, including market data subscriptions and event handling. IMessageProcessor Defines the contract for processing WebSocket messages from Kalshi's trading platform. Handles message routing, event raising, and queue management for real-time market data. ISubscriptionManager Defines the contract for managing WebSocket channel subscriptions to Kalshi's trading platform. Handles subscription lifecycle, state tracking, and market-specific channel management. IWebSocketConnectionManager Defines the contract for managing WebSocket connections to Kalshi's trading platform. Handles connection establishment, message sending/receiving, and reconnection logic.

api/KalshiBotAPI.Websockets.BaseSubscrip.summary: Class BaseSubscriptionManager Base class for managing WebSocket channel subscriptions for real-time market data from Kalshi's trading platform. Handles subscription lifecycle, state tracking, confirmation processing, and queue management for reliable market data streaming with proper error handling and recovery mechanisms. api/KalshiBotAPI.Websockets.KalshiWebSoc.summary: Class KalshiWebSocketClient Main WebSocket client for connecting to Kalshi's trading platform. Manages the complete lifecycle of WebSocket connections, subscriptions to market data channels, and real-time message processing. Acts as the central orchestrator between connection management, subscription handling, and message processing components. api/KalshiBotAPI.Websockets.MessageProce.title: Class MessageProcessor | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotAPI.Websockets.MessageProce.summary: Class MessageProcessor Processes incoming WebSocket messages from Kalshi's trading platform, routing them to appropriate handlers based on message type. Features configurable message batching for high-volume scenarios, performance metrics collection, sophisticated locking mechanisms, and enhanced message deduplication with warnings. Manages event counting, order book message queuing, and integrates with data persistence and API services for comprehensive market data processing. api/KalshiBotAPI.Websockets.WebSocketCon.title: Class WebSocketConnectionManager | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotAPI.Websockets.WebSocketCon.summary: Class WebSocketConnectionManager Manages WebSocket connections to Kalshi's trading platform with robust reconnection logic, authentication, and thread-safe operations. This class handles the low-level WebSocket communication layer, providing reliable connection management for real-time market data streaming. Inheritance object WebSocketConnectionManager Implements IWebSocketConnectionManager Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: KalshiBotAPI.Websockets Assembly: KalshiBotAPI.dll Syntax public class WebSocketConnectionManager : IWebSocketConnectionManager Remarks The WebSocketConnectionManager is responsible for: - Establishing authenticated WebSocket connections using RSA-based signatures - Managing connection lifecycle with automatic reconnection capabilities - Providing thread-safe message sending and receiving operations - Handling connection state monitoring and error recovery - Coordinating with higher-level components for message processing This class implements the IWebSocketConnectionManager interface and is designed to be used by higher-level orchestration components like KalshiWebSocketClient. It provides the foundation for reliable real-time communication with Kalshi's trading platform. Key features include: - Configurable retry logic with customizable delays and maximum attempts - Performance metrics collection (connection success rates, latency, message throughput) - Signature caching for reduced computational overhead with hit rate tracking - Configurable WebSocket buffer sizes with utilization monitoring - Semaphore-based synchronization to prevent concurrent connection attempts - Configurable reconnection behavior (can be disabled/enabled) - Connection failure reason tracking and diagnostics - Sliding window throughput calculation for accurate real-time metrics - Comprehensive logging for operational visibility - Proper resource cleanup and disposal Constructors | Edit this page View Source WebSocketConnectionManager(IOptions<KalshiConfig>, IOptions<WebSocketConnectionManagerConfig>, ILogger<WebSocketConnectionManager>, IPerformanceMonitor, IKalshiAPIService) Initializes a new instance of the WebSocketConnectionManager class. Declaration public WebSocketConnectionManager(IOptions<KalshiConfig> kalshiConfig, IOptions<WebSocketConnectionManagerConfig> websocketConfig, ILogger<WebSocketConnectionManager> logger, IPerformanceMonitor performanceMonitor, IKalshiAPIService kalshiApiService) Parameters Type Name Description IOptions<KalshiConfig> kalshiConfig Configuration options containing API credentials. IOptions<WebSocketConnectionManagerConfig> websocketConfig Configuration options for WebSocket operations. ILogger<WebSocketConnectionManager> logger Logger instance for recording connection operations and errors. IPerformanceMonitor performanceMonitor Optional performance monitor for recording WebSocket metrics. IKalshiAPIService kalshiApiService API service for checking exchange status and connectivity. Remarks The constructor sets up the RSA private key for authentication by loading it from the configured key file. This key is used to generate signatures for WebSocket connection authentication with Kalshi's servers. It also initializes all configurable parameters including retry delays, buffer sizes, timeouts, and cache durations for optimal performance. Properties | Edit this page View Source AverageBufferUtilization Gets the average buffer utilization (0.0 to 1.0). Declaration public double AverageBufferUtilization { get; } Property Value Type Description double Remarks Indicates how efficiently the WebSocket buffer is being used. Values closer to 1.0 suggest the buffer size is appropriate for the message sizes. Values much lower than 1.0 might indicate the buffer is oversized. | Edit this page View Source AverageConnectionLatency Gets the average connection latency in milliseconds. Declaration public double AverageConnectionLatency { get; } Property Value Type Description double | Edit this page View Source BandwidthBps Gets the current bandwidth utilization in bytes per second. Declaration public double BandwidthBps { get; } Property Value Type Description double Remarks Calculated as total bytes received divided by total connected time. | Edit this page View Source ConnectSemaphoreCount Gets the current count of the connection semaphore, indicating connection operation status. Declaration public int ConnectSemaphoreCount { get; } Property Value Type Description int Remarks The semaphore count indicates whether connection operations are currently in progress: - 1: No connection operation is active (semaphore is available) - 0: A connection operation is currently in progress (semaphore is held) This property is useful for monitoring and debugging connection concurrency issues. A count of 0 indicates that another thread is currently attempting to connect or reset the WebSocket connection. | Edit this page View Source ConnectionAttempts Gets the total number of connection attempts made. Declaration public int ConnectionAttempts { get; } Property Value Type Description int | Edit this page View Source ConnectionFailureReasons Gets the connection failure reasons and their counts. Declaration public IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> ConnectionFailureReasons { get; } Property Value Type Description IReadOnlyDictionary<string, int> Remarks Provides detailed breakdown of failure types (e.g., "WebSocketException", "TimeoutException") to help diagnose connection issues and optimize retry strategies. | Edit this page View Source ConnectionFailures Gets the total number of connection failures. Declaration public int ConnectionFailures { get; } Property Value Type Description int Remarks Tracks all connection failures for monitoring connection reliability and identifying potential network or configuration issues. | Edit this page View Source ConnectionSuccessRate Gets the connection success rate (0.0 to 1.0). Declaration public double ConnectionSuccessRate { get; } Property Value Type Description double | Edit this page View Source ConnectionSuccesses Gets the number of successful connections. Declaration public int ConnectionSuccesses { get; } Property Value Type Description int | Edit this page View Source EnableMetrics Gets or sets whether performance metrics collection is enabled. When disabled, metric tracking is skipped to reduce overhead. Declaration public bool EnableMetrics { get; set; } Property Value Type Description bool | Edit this page View Source ErrorRate Gets the error rate (0.0 to 1.0) for message processing. Declaration public double ErrorRate { get; } Property Value Type Description double Remarks Ratio of messages with errors to total messages processed. | Edit this page View Source IsExchangeOutage Gets whether the WebSocket connection is currently experiencing an exchange-wide outage. When true, connection issues are due to exchange problems rather than individual market issues. Declaration public bool IsExchangeOutage { get; } Property Value Type Description bool Remarks This flag is used to prevent marking individual markets as unhealthy during exchange-wide outages. Markets should only be marked unhealthy for individual market-specific issues, not exchange-wide problems. | Edit this page View Source MessageThroughput Gets the current message throughput (messages per second). Declaration public double MessageThroughput { get; } Property Value Type Description double Remarks Calculated using a sliding window approach over the last 60 seconds for more accurate real-time throughput measurement. | Edit this page View Source MessagesReceived Gets the total number of messages received. Declaration public int MessagesReceived { get; } Property Value Type Description int | Edit this page View Source QueueDepth Gets the current queue depth (number of messages waiting to be processed). Declaration public int QueueDepth { get; } Property Value Type Description int Remarks Currently returns 0 as messages are processed immediately without queuing. | Edit this page View Source ReconnectionCount Gets the number of reconnections performed. Declaration public int ReconnectionCount { get; } Property Value Type Description int | Edit this page View Source RoundTripTime Gets the round-trip time (RTT) in milliseconds (proxied by average connection latency). Declaration public double RoundTripTime { get; } Property Value Type Description double Remarks Since WebSocket is asynchronous and receive-only, this uses connection latency as a proxy for RTT. | Edit this page View Source SignatureCacheHitRate Gets the signature cache hit rate (0.0 to 1.0). Declaration public double SignatureCacheHitRate { get; } Property Value Type Description double Remarks Indicates the effectiveness of signature caching in reducing computational overhead. Higher values suggest better cache performance and reduced CPU usage for authentication. | Edit this page View Source SignatureCacheHits Gets the total number of signature cache hits. Declaration public int SignatureCacheHits { get; } Property Value Type Description int Remarks Tracks successful cache lookups that avoided expensive signature generation. | Edit this page View Source SignatureCacheMisses Gets the total number of signature cache misses. Declaration public int SignatureCacheMisses { get; } Property Value Type Description int Remarks Tracks cache misses that required new signature generation. | Edit this page View Source TotalBytesReceived Gets the total bytes received. Declaration public long TotalBytesReceived { get; } Property Value Type Description long Remarks Cumulative count of all data received through the WebSocket connection. Useful for monitoring data transfer volumes and connection efficiency. | Edit this page View Source TotalConnectedTimeMs Gets the total connected time in milliseconds. Declaration public long TotalConnectedTimeMs { get; } Property Value Type Description long Remarks Cumulative time the WebSocket has been connected across all sessions. Methods | Edit this page View Source ConnectAsync(int) Establishes a WebSocket connection to Kalshi's trading platform with automatic retry logic. Declaration public Task ConnectAsync(int retryCount = 0) Parameters Type Name Description int retryCount The current retry attempt number (used internally for exponential backoff). Returns Type Description Task A task representing the asynchronous connection operation. Remarks This method handles the complete WebSocket connection process including: - Checking reconnection permissions and current connection state - Acquiring connection semaphore to prevent concurrent connection attempts - Disposing of stale connections and creating new WebSocket instances - Setting up authentication headers with RSA signature generation - Establishing the connection with proper error handling The method implements configurable retry logic for connection failures, with configurable retry delays and maximum attempts. If all retries fail, it throws an InvalidOperationException with details about the connection failure. Thread Safety: This method is thread-safe and uses semaphores to prevent concurrent connection attempts that could cause race conditions. Exceptions Type Condition InvalidOperationException Thrown when the WebSocket connection cannot be established after all retry attempts. | Edit this page View Source DisableReconnect() Disables automatic reconnection for the WebSocket connection. Declaration public void DisableReconnect() Remarks When reconnection is disabled, the WebSocket will not attempt to automatically reconnect after connection failures. This is useful during application shutdown or when manual connection control is required. The current connection state remains unchanged until explicitly modified. | Edit this page View Source EnableReconnect() Enables automatic reconnection for the WebSocket connection. Declaration public void EnableReconnect() Remarks When reconnection is enabled, the WebSocket will automatically attempt to reconnect after connection failures using configurable retry logic. This is the default behavior for maintaining reliable connections to Kalshi's platform. | Edit this page View Source GetWebSocket() Gets the current WebSocket instance for direct access. Declaration public ClientWebSocket? GetWebSocket() Returns Type Description ClientWebSocket The current ClientWebSocket instance, or null if not connected. Remarks This method provides direct access to the underlying WebSocket for advanced operations that require low-level WebSocket manipulation. The returned instance should be used with caution as it bypasses the connection manager's thread-safety mechanisms. Thread Safety: This method is thread-safe and uses proper locking to ensure the WebSocket instance is not modified while being accessed. | Edit this page View Source IsConnected() Determines whether the WebSocket connection is currently active and ready for communication. Declaration public bool IsConnected() Returns Type Description bool True if the WebSocket is connected and in an open state, false otherwise. Remarks This method performs a comprehensive connection check by evaluating: - The internal connection flag (_isConnected) - The existence of a WebSocket instance - The WebSocket's current state (must be WebSocketState.Open) The method is thread-safe and provides detailed logging of the connection status for debugging purposes. It should be used to verify connection state before attempting to send messages or perform connection-dependent operations. | Edit this page View Source ReceiveAsync() Continuously receives messages from the WebSocket connection until the connection is closed or an error occurs. Declaration public Task ReceiveAsync() Returns Type Description Task A task representing the asynchronous receive operation. Remarks This method implements a continuous message receiving loop that: - Monitors the connection state and terminates if the WebSocket is no longer connected - Receives message fragments and assembles them into complete messages - Handles WebSocket close messages by throwing an exception to trigger reconnection - Processes only text messages, logging errors for unexpected message types - Maintains message ordering by waiting for EndOfMessage before processing The method includes comprehensive error handling for: - Connection loss during message reception - Server-initiated connection closures - Unexpected message types - General exceptions during the receive process Note: In the current implementation, received messages are logged but not processed. Message processing is handled by the higher-level MessageProcessor component that should be called from the main WebSocket client orchestrator. Exceptions Type Condition InvalidOperationException Thrown when the WebSocket connection is lost or closed by the server. | Edit this page View Source ResetConnectionAsync(bool) Resets the current WebSocket connection and establishes a new one. Declaration public Task ResetConnectionAsync(bool isExchangeOutage = false) Parameters Type Name Description bool isExchangeOutage Whether this reset is due to an exchange-wide outage (default: false). Returns Type Description Task A task representing the asynchronous reset operation. Remarks This method performs a clean reset of the WebSocket connection by: - Checking if a reconnection is already in progress to prevent duplicate operations - Closing the existing connection gracefully if it exists - Disposing of the old WebSocket instance - Establishing a new connection if reconnection is allowed The method includes proper error handling and ensures thread-safe operations during the connection reset process. If reconnection is disabled, the method will only clean up the existing connection without establishing a new one. When isExchangeOutage is true, this indicates the reset is due to exchange-wide issues rather than individual market problems, which affects how subscription health is monitored. | Edit this page View Source SendMessageAsync(string) Sends a text message through the WebSocket connection. Declaration public Task SendMessageAsync(string message) Parameters Type Name Description string message The message string to send. Returns Type Description Task A task representing the asynchronous send operation. Remarks This method ensures thread-safe message sending by: - Acquiring the WebSocket lock to prevent concurrent access - Verifying the connection is active before sending - Converting the message to UTF-8 bytes for transmission - Sending the message as a complete text frame If the WebSocket is not connected or in an invalid state, the method will throw an InvalidOperationException with details about the connection status. Exceptions Type Condition InvalidOperationException Thrown when attempting to send a message while the WebSocket is not connected. | Edit this page View Source StopAsync() Gracefully stops the WebSocket connection and disables reconnection. Declaration public Task StopAsync() Returns Type Description Task A task representing the asynchronous stop operation. Remarks This method performs a clean shutdown by: - Disabling automatic reconnection to prevent restart attempts - Closing the active WebSocket connection with a normal closure status - Disposing of the WebSocket instance to free resources - Updating the connection state to reflect the shutdown The method includes proper error handling to ensure the shutdown process completes even if individual steps fail. This is typically called during application shutdown or when manual connection control is required. Implements IWebSocketConnectionManager

api/KalshiBotAPI.Websockets.html.title: Namespace KalshiBotAPI.Websockets | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/KalshiBotAPI.Websockets.html.summary: Namespace KalshiBotAPI.Websockets Classes BaseSubscriptionManager Base class for managing WebSocket channel subscriptions for real-time market data from Kalshi's trading platform. Handles subscription lifecycle, state tracking, confirmation processing, and queue management for reliable market data streaming with proper error handling and recovery mechanisms. KalshiWebSocketClient Main WebSocket client for connecting to Kalshi's trading platform. Manages the complete lifecycle of WebSocket connections, subscriptions to market data channels, and real-time message processing. Acts as the central orchestrator between connection management, subscription handling, and message processing components. MessageProcessor Processes incoming WebSocket messages from Kalshi's trading platform, routing them to appropriate handlers based on message type. Features configurable message batching for high-volume scenarios, performance metrics collection, sophisticated locking mechanisms, and enhanced message deduplication with warnings. Manages event counting, order book message queuing, and integrates with data persistence and API services for comprehensive market data processing. OverseerSubscriptionManager Subscription manager specifically for the Overseer application. Only subscribes to fill and lifecycle channels, unlike the main bot which subscribes to all market channels. SubscriptionManager Manages WebSocket channel subscriptions for real-time market data from Kalshi's trading platform. Handles subscription lifecycle, state tracking, confirmation processing, and queue management for reliable market data streaming with proper error handling and recovery mechanisms. WebSocketConnectionManager Manages WebSocket connections to Kalshi's trading platform with robust reconnection logic, authentication, and thread-safe operations. This class handles the low-level WebSocket communication layer, providing reliable connection management for real-time market data streaming.

api/TradingSimulator.Simulator.Simulator.title: Class SimulatorReporting | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingSimulator.Simulator.Simulator.summary: Class SimulatorReporting Provides comprehensive reporting and analysis functionality for trading simulator operations. This class serves as the core engine for detecting velocity discrepancies in market snapshots, computing rolling observations, generating detailed memos, and coalescing discrepancy data. It is designed to analyze orderbook flow patterns and identify potential anomalies in market data that could indicate trading opportunities or data quality issues. Key Features: Configurable analysis parameters via AnalysisConfiguration class Async processing support for better performance with large datasets Parallel processing for multiple market analyses Comprehensive input validation for data integrity Performance metrics collection and timing analysis Configurable logging with automatic directory creation Spike suppression to reduce false positives Robust error handling and recovery Inheritance object SimulatorReporting Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingSimulator.Simulator Assembly: TradingSimulator.dll Syntax public class SimulatorReporting Constructors | Edit this page View Source SimulatorReporting(AnalysisConfiguration) Initializes a new instance of the SimulatorReporting class. Declaration public SimulatorReporting(AnalysisConfiguration config = null) Parameters Type Name Description AnalysisConfiguration config The analysis configuration to use. If null, default configuration is used. Methods | Edit this page View Source DetectVelocityDiscrepancies(List<MarketSnapshot>, bool) Detects velocity discrepancies in market snapshots by analyzing orderbook flow patterns. This method compares observed orderbook velocity changes against expected flows derived from rolling window analysis, identifying potential anomalies that may indicate trading opportunities or data quality issues. The analysis includes spike suppression, leakage tolerance, and various statistical thresholds to reduce false positives. Features: Automatic parallel processing for large datasets (>100 snapshots) Comprehensive input validation and error handling Performance metrics collection when enabled Configurable logging with automatic directory creation Robust spike suppression to reduce false positives Declaration public List<PricePoint> DetectVelocityDiscrepancies(List<MarketSnapshot> s, bool CreateLog) Parameters Type Name Description List<MarketSnapshot> s The list of market snapshots to analyze for velocity discrepancies. bool CreateLog Whether to create log files for detected discrepancies. Returns Type Description List<PricePoint> A list of PricePoint objects representing detected velocity discrepancies. | Edit this page View Source DetectVelocityDiscrepanciesAsync(List<MarketSnapshot>, bool) Asynchronously detects velocity discrepancies in market snapshots by analyzing orderbook flow patterns. This method provides the same functionality as the synchronous version but runs on a background thread to avoid blocking the calling thread. Ideal for large datasets or when responsiveness is critical. Declaration public Task<List<PricePoint>> DetectVelocityDiscrepanciesAsync(List<MarketSnapshot> s, bool CreateLog) Parameters Type Name Description List<MarketSnapshot> s The list of market snapshots to analyze for velocity discrepancies. bool CreateLog Whether to create log files for detected discrepancies. Returns Type Description Task<List<PricePoint>> A task that represents the asynchronous operation, containing a list of PricePoint objects representing detected velocity discrepancies. | Edit this page View Source GetPerformanceMetrics() Gets the current performance metrics for the analysis. Declaration public PerformanceMetrics GetPerformanceMetrics() Returns Type Description PerformanceMetrics The performance metrics instance.

api/TradingSimulator.Strategies.TradingD.title: Class TradingDecision | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingSimulator.Strategies.TradingD.summary: Class TradingDecision Represents a trading decision generated by a trading strategy evaluation. This class encapsulates the signals, metadata, and finality status of a decision made during backtesting or live trading simulation. It serves as the primary output container for strategy logic, allowing strategies to communicate actionable insights and supplementary information to the trading engine. api/TradingSimulator.Strategies.TradingD.title: Enum TradingDecisionEnum | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingSimulator.Strategies.TradingD.summary: Enum TradingDecisionEnum Defines the possible trading decisions that can be made by a trading strategy. This enum is used throughout the trading simulator to represent the core actions a strategy can recommend: purchasing an asset, maintaining the current position, or selling an existing position. It serves as the fundamental decision type for strategy evaluation and trade execution logic. Namespace: TradingSimulator.Strategies Assembly: TradingSimulator.dll Syntax public enum TradingDecisionEnum Fields Name Description Buy Indicates a recommendation to purchase or enter a long position in the asset. This decision is typically generated when strategy signals suggest upward price movement or favorable market conditions for buying. Hold Indicates a recommendation to maintain the current position without making changes. This decision is used when strategy signals do not strongly suggest buying or selling, or when the market conditions are neutral or uncertain. Sell Indicates a recommendation to sell or exit a long position in the asset. This decision is typically generated when strategy signals suggest downward price movement or unfavorable market conditions for holding the current position.

api/TradingSimulator.TradingSimulatorSer.summary: Class TradingSimulatorService Core service for orchestrating trading strategy simulations and backtesting operations. This service manages the complete lifecycle of running trading strategies against historical market snapshots, including data loading, strategy execution, performance analysis, and result reporting. It integrates with various components like DataLoader, MarketProcessor, and StrategyResolver to provide comprehensive simulation capabilities for evaluating trading strategies. Features include configurable cache directory and timeout values, input validation with warnings for invalid parameters, and async operations for high-throughput scenarios. api/TradingSimulator.html.title: Namespace TradingSimulator | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingSimulator.html.summary: Namespace TradingSimulator Classes CachedMarketData Contains cached market data for a specific market, including profit/loss, position information, and various lists of price points representing different market events and states. This class serves as the primary data structure for storing processed market simulation results that can be serialized to JSON for persistence and later visualization in the GUI. Enhanced with validation, deep cloning, and serialization controls. DataLoader Provides functionality for loading and filtering market snapshot data for the trading simulator. MarketProcessor Processes individual markets in the trading simulator by running trading strategies, analyzing event logs, and generating visualization data points for market analysis. This class orchestrates the complete market processing pipeline from simulation to data persistence. PerformanceMetrics Performance metrics class for tracking message processing rates and queue depths. PricePoint Represents a point in time with a price value and optional memo for market data visualization. This record is used throughout the trading simulator to store timestamped price information with associated metadata for charting and analysis purposes. StrategyResolver Central resolver for trading strategy families in the trading simulator. This class acts as a bridge between strategy family enums and their concrete implementations, providing a unified interface for accessing training mappings and parameter sets for different trading strategies. It encapsulates the logic for resolving strategy families to their corresponding configurations and mappings. TradePoint Represents a trade point with timestamp, price, and trading decision. This record encapsulates the essential data for a trading action in the simulator. TradingContext Context object containing shared variables and trading decisions for strategy execution. TradingSimulatorService Core service for orchestrating trading strategy simulations and backtesting operations. This service manages the complete lifecycle of running trading strategies against historical market snapshots, including data loading, strategy execution, performance analysis, and result reporting. It integrates with various components like DataLoader, MarketProcessor, and StrategyResolver to provide comprehensive simulation capabilities for evaluating trading strategies. Features include configurable cache directory and timeout values, input validation with warnings for invalid parameters, and async operations for high-throughput scenarios. Enums TradingSimulatorService.StrategyFamily Enumeration of available trading strategy families for simulation. Delegates TradingStrategyFunc<T> Delegate for trading strategy functions that process market data snapshots.

api/TradingStrategies.Extensions.MarketS.title: Class MarketSnapshotExtensions | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Extensions.MarketS.summary: Class MarketSnapshotExtensions Provides extension methods for MarketSnapshot to compute and update order book metrics from simulated order book data. This class serves as a bridge between the raw SimulatedOrderbook data structure and the calculated metrics stored in MarketSnapshot, enabling efficient computation of spreads, depths, volumes, and other trading indicators. All calculations are performed in a stateless manner, ensuring thread safety and reusability across different market simulation scenarios. Performance monitoring can be configured via MarketSnapshotExtensions:EnablePerformanceMetrics setting. Inheritance object MarketSnapshotExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Extensions Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public static class MarketSnapshotExtensions Methods | Edit this page View Source UpdateOrderbookMetricsFromSimulated(MarketSnapshot, SimulatedOrderbook) Updates all order book-related metrics in the MarketSnapshot based on the current state of the SimulatedOrderbook. This method recalculates spreads, depths, volumes, ranges, imbalances, and center of mass values to reflect the latest order book state. It is typically called after order book modifications during trading simulations to ensure all derived metrics remain consistent with the underlying order book data. Includes input validation and performance timing for robustness and monitoring. Declaration public static void UpdateOrderbookMetricsFromSimulated(this MarketSnapshot snapshot, SimulatedOrderbook book) Parameters Type Name Description MarketSnapshot snapshot The MarketSnapshot instance whose metrics will be updated. SimulatedOrderbook book The SimulatedOrderbook containing the current order book state to analyze. Remarks This method performs comprehensive order book analysis including: Best bid/ask price identification Spread calculations between bid and ask prices Depth analysis at best prices and within tolerance ranges Volume calculations in both contract count and dollar value Price range analysis for bid orders Imbalance calculations between yes/no sides Center of mass calculations for order book distribution All calculations use the snapshot's TolerancePercentage for range-based metrics and assume prices are in cents (1-99 range) as per Kalshi market conventions. Includes input validation to prevent null reference exceptions and performance timing measurement for monitoring execution time in high-frequency scenarios.

api/TradingStrategies.Extensions.PseudoC.title: Class PseudoCandlestickExtensions | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Extensions.PseudoC.summary: Class PseudoCandlestickExtensions Provides extension methods for converting PseudoCandlestick collections to standard candlestick formats. This class serves as a bridge between simplified mid-price candlestick data and traditional OHLC candlestick data, enabling pattern detection and technical analysis algorithms to work with market data. Inheritance object PseudoCandlestickExtensions Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Extensions Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public static class PseudoCandlestickExtensions Methods | Edit this page View Source ToCandleMids(IList<PseudoCandlestick>, string, ILogger) Converts a sequence of PseudoCandlesticks to an array of CandleMids with OHLC data. Each resulting candlestick represents the price movement between consecutive PseudoCandlesticks, using the previous candlestick's mid-close as the open price and the current candlestick's mid-close as the close price, with high/low from the current candlestick. Declaration public static CandleMids[] ToCandleMids(this IList<PseudoCandlestick> candles, string marketTicker, ILogger logger = null) Parameters Type Name Description IList<PseudoCandlestick> candles The sequence of PseudoCandlesticks to convert. Must contain at least 2 elements. string marketTicker The market ticker identifier to assign to each resulting CandleMids. ILogger logger Optional logger for performance metrics collection. Returns Type Description CandleMids[] An array of CandleMids representing the price movements between consecutive input candlesticks. Remarks This method creates n-1 candlesticks from n input PseudoCandlesticks, where each output candlestick represents the transition from one time period to the next. The volume is cast from decimal to double to match the CandleMids data type expectations, with optional precision rounding based on config. Performance metrics are logged if the conversion takes longer than 100ms for large sequences. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException Thrown when candles is null. ArgumentException Thrown when candles contains fewer than 2 elements or marketTicker is null/empty.

api/TradingStrategies.Extensions.html.title: Namespace TradingStrategies.Extensions | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Extensions.html.summary: Namespace TradingStrategies.Extensions Classes MarketSnapshotExtensions Provides extension methods for MarketSnapshot to compute and update order book metrics from simulated order book data. This class serves as a bridge between the raw SimulatedOrderbook data structure and the calculated metrics stored in MarketSnapshot, enabling efficient computation of spreads, depths, volumes, and other trading indicators. All calculations are performed in a stateless manner, ensuring thread safety and reusability across different market simulation scenarios. Performance monitoring can be configured via MarketSnapshotExtensions:EnablePerformanceMetrics setting. PseudoCandlestickExtensions Provides extension methods for converting PseudoCandlestick collections to standard candlestick formats. This class serves as a bridge between simplified mid-price candlestick data and traditional OHLC candlestick data, enabling pattern detection and technical analysis algorithms to work with market data.

api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Signal..title: Enum Signal | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Signal..summary: Enum Signal Defines the various trading signals used by the strategy system to identify market conditions and technical indicators. These signals represent different market states, technical analysis patterns, and external factors that can influence trading decisions. Each signal corresponds to a specific condition that can be evaluated against market data to determine appropriate trading actions. Namespace: TradingStrategies.Strategies Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public enum Signal Fields Name Description AbsPositionAboveMax Absolute position size is above the maximum allowed threshold. ActiveYes Yes side showing active trading interest. AtrMediumHigh Average True Range (ATR) indicates medium to high volatility. BearishMomentum Bearish momentum indicators are present. BearishVolume Bearish volume patterns detected. BidImbalance Bid side shows imbalance compared to ask side. BreakoutDown Price breaking out to the downside. BreakoutUp Price breaking out to the upside. BullishMomentum Bullish momentum indicators are present. BullishVolume Bullish volume patterns detected. BuyFlow Strong buying flow detected in the market. BuyPressure Strong buying pressure detected. CryptoNews Cryptocurrency-related news impacting markets. DepthAtBestYesAskLow Depth at the best Yes ask is low, indicating thin liquidity on the sell side. DepthAtBestYesBidLow Depth at the best Yes bid is low, indicating thin liquidity on the buy side. ElectionNearing Election timing is nearing, potentially affecting market volatility. ExtremeWeather Extreme weather conditions affecting market sentiment. FadeHigh Price fading from recent highs. FadeLow Price fading from recent lows. HighVolBuy High volume buying activity detected. HighVolSell High volume selling activity detected. LargeBuyTrades Large buy trades executed. LiveEvent Live event occurring that may impact market outcomes. MacdAboveSignal MACD line is above the signal line, indicating bullish momentum. MacdBelowSignal MACD line is below the signal line, indicating bearish momentum. MacroEvent Macroeconomic event influencing market conditions. MidAboveBollingerUpper Mid price is above the upper Bollinger Band, suggesting potential reversal or overextension. MidAboveEmaMedium Mid price is above the medium-term EMA. MidAboveEmaPlusAtr Mid price is above the EMA plus ATR band, indicating potential overbought conditions. MidAboveVwap Mid price is above the Volume Weighted Average Price (VWAP). MidBelowBollingerLower Mid price is below the lower Bollinger Band, suggesting potential reversal or overextension. MidBelowEmaMedium Mid price is below the medium-term EMA. MidBelowEmaMinusAtr Mid price is below the EMA minus ATR band, indicating potential oversold conditions. MidBelowVwap Mid price is below the Volume Weighted Average Price (VWAP). NearResistance Price is near a resistance level, potentially indicating a reversal opportunity. NearSupport Price is near a support level, potentially indicating a bounce opportunity. NegativeHistogram MACD histogram shows negative divergence. NegativeOBV On-Balance Volume (OBV) shows negative divergence. ObvMedium On-Balance Volume (OBV) indicates medium-term accumulation/distribution. Overbought Market indicators suggest overbought conditions. Oversold Market indicators suggest oversold conditions. PositiveHistogram MACD histogram shows positive divergence. PositiveOBV On-Balance Volume (OBV) shows positive divergence. RateCutExpectation Market expectation of interest rate cuts. RsiShort RSI indicates short-term overbought conditions. SeasonalPattern Seasonal pattern influencing market behavior. SellFlow Strong selling flow detected in the market. SellPressure Strong selling pressure detected. StochasticK Stochastic K oscillator reading. TeamFavorite Team or competitor favoritism influencing market sentiment. TradeVolumePerMinuteNoHigh Trade volume per minute for No contracts is high. TradeVolumePerMinuteYesHigh Trade volume per minute for Yes contracts is high. UndervaluedCandidate Asset appears to be undervalued relative to fundamentals. VelocityPerMinuteTopNoBid Velocity per minute at the top No bid price level. VelocityPerMinuteTopYesBid Velocity per minute at the top Yes bid price level. WideSpreadBuy Wide spread conditions favoring buy orders. WideSpreadSell Wide spread conditions favoring sell orders.

api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.SignalC.title: Class SignalCondition | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.SignalC.summary: Class SignalCondition Defines a condition based on a trading signal that triggers a specific trading action. This class combines a signal (technical indicator or market condition), a comparison operator, a threshold value, and the resulting action type to create rule-based trading logic. Signal conditions are used by strategies to evaluate market states and determine appropriate responses. api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Strat.h.summary: Class Strat Abstract base class for individual trading strategy implementations. Concrete strategy classes inherit from this class to provide specific trading logic that evaluates market conditions and returns trading decisions. Each strategy has a weight that influences its importance when combined with other strategies in a composite approach. api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Strateg.title: Class Strategy | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.Strateg.summary: Class Strategy Represents a composite trading strategy that combines multiple individual strategies (Strats). This class aggregates the decisions from multiple strategies based on their weights, implementing a voting system where the strategy with the highest combined weight for a particular action wins. The composite approach allows for more robust trading decisions by considering multiple perspectives. Inheritance object Strategy Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: TradingStrategies.Strategies Assembly: TradingStrategies.dll Syntax public class Strategy Constructors | Edit this page View Source Strategy(string, List<Strat>) Initializes a new instance of the Strategy class with a name and list of individual strategies. Declaration public Strategy(string name, List<Strat> strats) Parameters Type Name Description string name The name identifier for this strategy. List<Strat> strats The list of individual strategies to combine in this composite strategy. Properties | Edit this page View Source Name Gets the name of this strategy for identification and logging purposes. Declaration public string Name { get; } Property Value Type Description string | Edit this page View Source Strats Gets the read-only list of individual strategies that make up this composite strategy. Declaration public IReadOnlyList<Strat> Strats { get; } Property Value Type Description IReadOnlyList<Strat> Methods | Edit this page View Source GetAction(MarketSnapshot, MarketSnapshot?, int) Evaluates all individual strategies and returns the aggregated trading decision. Uses a weighted voting system where each strategy's weight contributes to its action type. The action type with the highest total weight is selected, and among strategies voting for that type, the one with the highest individual weight provides the final decision details. Declaration public ActionDecision GetAction(MarketSnapshot snapshot, MarketSnapshot? previousSnapshot, int simulationPosition = 0) Parameters Type Name Description MarketSnapshot snapshot The current market snapshot containing price, volume, and technical indicator data. MarketSnapshot previousSnapshot The previous market snapshot for comparison, or null if this is the first snapshot. int simulationPosition The current position in the simulation (0 for no position, positive for long, negative for short). Returns Type Description ActionDecision An ActionDecision containing the recommended trading action and associated metadata from the highest-weighted strategy.

api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.html.title: Namespace TradingStrategies.Strategies | Kalshi Trading Bot Documentation
api/TradingStrategies.Strategies.html.summary: Namespace TradingStrategies.Strategies Classes Scenario Represents a trading scenario that organizes strategies based on different market conditions. This class serves as a container for mapping market types to their corresponding trading strategies, allowing the system to select appropriate strategies based on current market conditions during simulation or live trading. SignalCondition Defines a condition based on a trading signal that triggers a specific trading action. This class combines a signal (technical indicator or market condition), a comparison operator, a threshold value, and the resulting action type to create rule-based trading logic. Signal conditions are used by strategies to evaluate market states and determine appropriate responses. Strat Abstract base class for individual trading strategy implementations. Concrete strategy classes inherit from this class to provide specific trading logic that evaluates market conditions and returns trading decisions. Each strategy has a weight that influences its importance when combined with other strategies in a composite approach. Strategy Represents a composite trading strategy that combines multiple individual strategies (Strats). This class aggregates the decisions from multiple strategies based on their weights, implementing a voting system where the strategy with the highest combined weight for a particular action wins. The composite approach allows for more robust trading decisions by considering multiple perspectives. Enums Signal Defines the various trading signals used by the strategy system to identify market conditions and technical indicators. These signals represent different market states, technical analysis patterns, and external factors that can influence trading decisions. Each signal corresponds to a specific condition that can be evaluated against market data to determine appropriate trading actions.
